Detailed Description

Represents a graph structure used for bucket-based optimization in a solver.

The BucketGraph class provides various functionalities for managing and optimizing a graph structure using buckets. It includes methods for initialization, configuration printing, node visitation checks, statistics printing, bucket assignment, and more.

The class also supports parallel arc generation, label management, and feasibility checks. It is designed to work with different directions (Forward and Backward) and stages of the optimization process.

Note
This class relies on several preprocessor directives (e.g., RIH, RCC, SRC) to conditionally enable or disable certain features.
Template Parameters
DirectionThe direction of the graph (Forward or Backward).
StageThe stage of the optimization process.
FullIndicates whether the full labeling algorithm should be used.
ArcTypeThe type of arc (Bucket or regular).
MutabilityIndicates whether the label is mutable or immutable.

BucketGraph::BucketGraph ( const std::vector< VRPNode > & nodes,
int horizon,
int bucket_interval,
int capacity,
int capacity_interval )

Constructs a BucketGraph object.

This constructor initializes a BucketGraph with the given nodes, time horizon, and bucket interval. It sets up the forward and backward buckets, initializes the dual values for the CVRP separation, and defines the intervals and resource limits.

◆ assign_buckets() [1/2]

template<Direction D>
auto & BucketGraph::assign_buckets ( auto & FW,
auto & BW )
inlineconstexprnoexcept

Assigns the buckets based on the specified direction.

This function returns a reference to the buckets based on the specified direction. If the direction is Forward, it returns a reference to the forward buckets. If the direction is Backward, it returns a reference to the backward buckets.

Template Parameters
DThe direction (Forward or Backward).
Parameters
FWThe forward buckets.
BWThe backward buckets.
Returns
A reference to the buckets based on the specified direction.

◆ augment_ng_memories()

void BucketGraph::augment_ng_memories ( std::vector< double > & solution,
std::vector< Path > & paths,
bool aggressive,
int eta1,
int eta2,
int eta_max,
int nC )

Augments the memories in the BucketGraph.

This function takes a solution vector, a SparseModel, and several parameters to augment the memories in the BucketGraph. It identifies cycles in the SparseModel that meet certain conditions and forbids them in the BucketGraph. The function prioritizes smaller cycles and limits the number of forbidden cycles based on the given parameters.

Definition at line 325 of file BucketGraph.cpp.

326 {
327 // Pre-allocate an estimated number of cycles.
328 std::vector<CycleData> cycles;
329 cycles.reserve(paths.size() / 4);
330 ng_iteration_counter++;
331
332 // Detect cycles in fractional paths.
333 for (size_t pathIndex = 0; pathIndex < paths.size(); ++pathIndex) {
334 const double epsilon = 1e-3;
335 double sol_val = solution[pathIndex];
336 if (sol_val <= epsilon) continue;
337 const auto &path = paths[pathIndex];
338 ankerl::unordered_dense::map<uint16_t, int> visited_clients;
339 visited_clients.reserve(path.size());
340
341 // Iterate over the nodes in the path to detect cycles.
342 for (size_t i = 0; i < path.size(); ++i) {
343 uint16_t client = path[i];
344 // Skip depot nodes.
345 if (client == 0 || client == N_SIZE - 1) continue;
346 auto it = visited_clients.find(client);
347 if (it != visited_clients.end()) {
348 // Cycle found: extract cycle from first occurrence to current
349 // index.
350 std::vector<uint16_t> cycle;
351 cycle.reserve(i - it->second + 1);
352 for (size_t j = it->second; j <= i; ++j) cycle.push_back(path[j]);
353
354 CycleData data;
355 data.cycle = std::move(cycle);
356 // Option 1: Use raw solution value as violation score.
357 // data.metric = sol_val;
358 // Option 2: Use fractionality (how far is sol_val from an
359 // integer).
360 data.metric = std::min(sol_val, 1.0 - sol_val);
361 cycles.push_back(std::move(data));
362 break; // Process only one cycle per path.
363 }
364 visited_clients[client] = i;
365 }
366 }
367 if (cycles.empty()) return;
368
369 // Sort cycles by descending metric value (i.e. cycles with highest
370 // violation or fractionality first).
371 pdqsort(cycles.begin(), cycles.end(), [](const CycleData &a, const CycleData &b) { return a.metric > b.metric; });
372
373 int forbidden_count = 0;
374
375 // Cache neighborhood bit counts to avoid redundant __builtin_popcountll
376 // calls.
377 std::vector<int> cached_counts(N_SIZE, -1);
378 auto count_neighborhood_bits = [this, eta_max, &cached_counts](uint16_t node) -> int {
379 if (cached_counts[node] >= 0) return cached_counts[node];
380 int count = 0;
381 for (const auto &segment : neighborhoods_bitmap[node]) {
382 count += __builtin_popcountll(segment);
383 if (count >= eta_max) break;
384 }
385 cached_counts[node] = count;
386 return count;
387 };
388
389 // Adapt thresholds based on the aggressive flag.
390 int effective_eta1 = eta1;
391 int effective_eta2 = eta2;
392
393 // Process each detected cycle.
394 for (const auto &data : cycles) {
395 const auto &cycle = data.cycle;
396 if (cycle.empty()) continue;
397
398 bool can_forbid = true;
399 // Check if any node in the cycle has too many neighbor bits.
400 for (uint16_t node : cycle) {
401 if (count_neighborhood_bits(node) >= eta_max) {
402 can_forbid = false;
403 break;
404 }
405 }
406
407 // If the cycle qualifies based on its size or our current count.
408 if (can_forbid && (static_cast<int>(cycle.size()) <= effective_eta1 || forbidden_count < effective_eta2)) {
409 std::vector<int> int_cycle(cycle.begin(), cycle.end());
410 forbidCycle(int_cycle, aggressive);
411 ++forbidden_count;
412 if (forbidden_count >= effective_eta2) break;
413 }
414 }
415 // If no cycle was added, try again without the effective_eta1 restriction.
416 if (forbidden_count == 0) {
417 for (const auto &data : cycles) {
418 const auto &cycle = data.cycle;
419 if (cycle.empty()) continue;
420
421 bool can_forbid = true;
422 for (uint16_t node : cycle) {
423 if (count_neighborhood_bits(node) >= eta_max) {
424 can_forbid = false;
425 break;
426 }
427 }
428 if (can_forbid && forbidden_count < effective_eta2) {
429 std::vector<int> int_cycle(cycle.begin(), cycle.end());
430 forbidCycle(int_cycle, aggressive);
431 ++forbidden_count;
432 if (forbidden_count >= effective_eta2) break;
433 }
434 }
435 }
436}

◆ bucket_fixing()

template<Stage S>
void BucketGraph::bucket_fixing ( )

Fixes the bucket arcs for the specified stage.

This function performs the bucket arc fixing for the given stage. It initializes necessary variables and runs labeling algorithms to compute forward and backward reduced costs. Based on the computed gap, it performs arc elimination in both forward and backward directions and generates the necessary arcs.

Definition at line 460 of file BucketUtils.h.

460 {
461 // Only execute if not already fixed.
462 if (!fixed) {
463 fmt::print("\033[34m_STARTING BUCKET FIXING PROCEDURE\033[0m\n");
464 fixed = true;
465
466 // Compute gap and check feasibility (if gap is negative, exit
467 // early).
468 gap = std::ceil(incumbent - (relaxation + std::min(0.0, min_red_cost)));
469 if (gap < 0) {
470 fmt::print("\033[34m_BUCKET FIXING PROCEDURE CAN'T BE EXECUTED DUE TO "
471 "GAP\033[0m\n");
472 fmt::print("gap: {}\n", gap);
473 return;
474 }
475
476 // Initialize common structures, arc scores, warm labels, etc.
477 common_initialization();
478
479 // Initialize forward and backward c_bar vectors.
480 std::vector<double> forward_cbar(fw_buckets.size(), std::numeric_limits<double>::infinity());
481 std::vector<double> backward_cbar(bw_buckets.size(), std::numeric_limits<double>::infinity());
482
483 // Run labeling algorithms for Stage Four with full mode.
484 run_labeling_algorithms<Stage::Four, Full::Full>(forward_cbar, backward_cbar);
485 // Save computed c_bar values.
486 fw_c_bar = forward_cbar;
487 bw_c_bar = backward_cbar;
488
489 // Print info about bucket arc elimination.
490 print_info("performing bucket arc elimination with theta = {}\n", gap);
491
492 // Run forward and backward sections in parallel.
493 PARALLEL_SECTIONS(
494 work, bi_sched,
495 SECTION {
496 // Section 1: Process forward direction.
497 BucketArcElimination<Direction::Forward>(gap);
498 ObtainJumpBucketArcs<Direction::Forward>();
499 },
500 SECTION {
501 // Section 2: Process backward direction.
502 BucketArcElimination<Direction::Backward>(gap);
503 ObtainJumpBucketArcs<Direction::Backward>();
504 });
505
506 // Generate arcs after elimination.
507 // generate_arcs();
508 fmt::print("\033[34m_BUCKET FIXING PROCEDURE FINISHED\033[0m\n");
509
510 just_fixed = true;
511 return;
512 }
513 just_fixed = false;
514}

◆ BucketArcElimination()

template<Direction D>
void BucketGraph::BucketArcElimination ( double theta)

Eliminates bucket arcs based on a given threshold.

This function performs bucket arc elimination in either the forward or backward direction, depending on the template parameter D. It resets all elements in the fixed buckets to 0, prints information about the direction of elimination, and processes arcs to update the local bucket arc map and fixed buckets.

Definition at line 166 of file BucketJump.h.

166 {
167 // Select direction-specific containers.
168 auto &buckets = assign_buckets<D>(fw_buckets, bw_buckets);
169 auto &Phi = assign_buckets<D>(Phi_fw, Phi_bw);
170 auto &Phi_opposite = assign_buckets<D>(Phi_bw, Phi_fw);
171 auto &buckets_size = assign_buckets<D>(fw_buckets_size, bw_buckets_size);
172 auto &fixed_buckets_bitmap = assign_buckets<D>(fw_fixed_buckets_bitmap, bw_fixed_buckets_bitmap);
173
174 // Reset fixed buckets bitmap.
175 fixed_buckets_bitmap.assign(fixed_buckets_bitmap.size(), 0);
176 const int n_buckets = buckets_size;
177 // Compute the number of 64-bit segments for bitmaps.
178 const size_t n_segments = (buckets_size + 63) / 64;
179
180 // Define ArcMap to store arc information.
181 using ArcMap = ankerl::unordered_dense::map<std::pair<std::pair<int, int>, int>, ankerl::unordered_dense::set<int>,
182 arc_map_hash>;
183 ArcMap local_B_Ba_b;
184 int removed_arcs = 0;
185
186 // Helper: create an arc key from two node IDs and a bucket.
187 auto create_arc_key = [](int from, int to, int b) { return std::make_pair(std::make_pair(from, to), b); };
188
189 // Helper lambda to quickly reset a bitmap vector.
190 auto reset_bitmap = [&](std::vector<uint64_t> &bitmap) {
191 // Using memset since our vector is contiguous.
192 if (!bitmap.empty()) std::memset(bitmap.data(), 0, bitmap.size() * sizeof(uint64_t));
193 };
194
195 // Check if there exists b' in BB such that b' <= bb_arr in the preorder
196 // induced by Phi_opposite (i.e., reachable from bb_arr through Phi*).
197 auto has_compatible_predecessor = [&](int bb_arr, const ankerl::unordered_dense::set<int> &BB) -> bool {
198 if (BB.find(bb_arr) != BB.end()) return true;
199
200 std::vector<int> stack;
201 stack.push_back(bb_arr);
202 std::vector<uint64_t> visited(n_segments, 0ULL);
203
204 while (!stack.empty()) {
205 const int curr = stack.back();
206 stack.pop_back();
207 const size_t seg = static_cast<size_t>(curr) >> 6;
208 const uint64_t mask = 1ULL << (curr & 63);
209 if (visited[seg] & mask) continue;
210 visited[seg] |= mask;
211
212 if (BB.find(curr) != BB.end()) return true;
213
214 for (int pred : Phi_opposite[curr]) {
215 const size_t pseg = static_cast<size_t>(pred) >> 6;
216 const uint64_t pmask = 1ULL << (pred & 63);
217 if ((visited[pseg] & pmask) == 0) { stack.push_back(pred); }
218 }
219 }
220 return false;
221 };
222
223 // Lambda: Process jump arcs for bucket 'b'.
224 auto process_jump_arcs = [&](int b) {
225 const auto &bucket_arcs = buckets[b].template get_bucket_arcs<D>();
226 if (bucket_arcs.empty()) return;
227
228 std::vector<uint64_t> Bvisited(n_segments, 0);
229 const auto &labels = buckets[b].get_labels();
230 const size_t n_main = options.main_resources.size();
231
232 for (const auto &a : bucket_arcs) {
233 if (!a.jump) continue;
234 const int to_node = a.jump_to_node >= 0 ? a.jump_to_node : buckets[a.to_bucket].node_id;
235 if (to_node < 0) continue;
236
237 std::vector<double> arrival(n_main, 0.0);
238 for (size_t r = 0; r < n_main; ++r) {
239 if constexpr (D == Direction::Forward) {
240 arrival[r] =
241 std::max(buckets[a.to_bucket].lb[r] + a.resource_increment[r], nodes[to_node].lb[r]);
242 } else {
243 arrival[r] =
244 std::min(buckets[a.to_bucket].ub[r] - a.resource_increment[r], nodes[to_node].ub[r]);
245 }
246 }
247
248 auto arc_key = create_arc_key(buckets[a.from_bucket].node_id, to_node, b);
249 int b_opposite = (D == Direction::Forward) ? get_bucket_number<Direction::Backward>(to_node, arrival)
250 : get_bucket_number<Direction::Forward>(to_node, arrival);
251 auto &Bidi_map = local_B_Ba_b[arc_key];
252
253 // Process each label in the current bucket.
254 for (auto &L_item : labels) {
255 if (L_item == nullptr || L_item->is_dominated) continue;
256
257 // Reset Bvisited for each UpdateBucketsSet call.
258 reset_bitmap(Bvisited);
259 UpdateBucketsSet<D>(theta, L_item, Bidi_map, b_opposite, Bvisited);
260 }
261 }
262 };
263
264 // Lambda: Process bucket arcs for bucket 'b'.
265 auto process_bucket_arcs = [&](int b) {
266 const auto &bucket_arcs = buckets[b].template get_bucket_arcs<D>();
267 if (bucket_arcs.empty()) return;
268
269 const auto &labels = buckets[b].get_labels();
270 std::vector<uint64_t> Bvisited(n_segments, 0);
271 const size_t n_main = options.main_resources.size();
272
273 for (const auto &a : bucket_arcs) {
274 if (a.jump) continue;
275
276 // Build the resource increment vector.
277 std::vector<double> increment(n_main, 0.0);
278 for (size_t r = 0; r < n_main; ++r) {
279 if constexpr (D == Direction::Forward)
280 increment[r] = buckets[b].lb[r] + a.resource_increment[r];
281 else
282 increment[r] = buckets[b].ub[r] - a.resource_increment[r];
283 }
284
285 auto arc_key = create_arc_key(buckets[a.from_bucket].node_id, buckets[a.to_bucket].node_id, b);
286 int b_opposite = get_opposite_bucket_number<D>(a.to_bucket, increment);
287 auto &Bidi_map = local_B_Ba_b[arc_key];
288
289 // Process each label in bucket 'b'.
290 for (auto &L_item : labels) {
291 if (L_item == nullptr || L_item->is_dominated) continue;
292
293 reset_bitmap(Bvisited);
294 UpdateBucketsSet<D>(theta, L_item, Bidi_map, b_opposite, Bvisited);
295 }
296
297 // Fix gamma if there is no compatible predecessor bucket in BB.
298 if (!has_compatible_predecessor(b_opposite, Bidi_map)) {
299 const size_t bit_pos = static_cast<size_t>(a.from_bucket) * n_buckets + a.to_bucket;
300 fixed_buckets_bitmap[bit_pos / 64] |= (1ULL << (bit_pos % 64));
301 ++removed_arcs;
302 }
303 }
304 };
305
306 // Process each bucket.
307 for (int b = 0; b < buckets_size; ++b) {
308 // Process node arcs.
309 const auto &node_arcs = nodes[buckets[b].node_id].template get_arcs<D>();
310 for (const auto &a : node_arcs) {
311 auto arc_key = std::make_pair(std::make_pair(a.from, a.to), b);
312 auto &Bidi_map = local_B_Ba_b[arc_key];
313
314 if (!Phi[b].empty()) {
315 // Merge neighbor bucket arcs.
316 for (const auto &b_prime : Phi[b]) {
317 auto neighbor_key = std::make_pair(std::make_pair(a.from, a.to), b_prime);
318 if (auto it = local_B_Ba_b.find(neighbor_key); it != local_B_Ba_b.end()) {
319 Bidi_map.insert(it->second.begin(), it->second.end());
320 }
321 }
322 }
323 }
324
325 // Process jump arcs and bucket arcs.
326 process_jump_arcs(b);
327 process_bucket_arcs(b);
328
329 // iterate over fixed buckets and remove them
330 for (int b_prime = 0; b_prime < buckets_size; ++b_prime) {
331 if (is_bucket_fixed<D>(b, b_prime)) { buckets[b].template remove_bucket_arc<D>(b, b_prime); }
332 }
333 }
334
335 // Print the total number of removed arcs.
336 if constexpr (D == Direction::Forward)

◆ calculate_neighborhoods()

void BucketGraph::calculate_neighborhoods ( size_t num_closest)

Calculates the neighborhoods for each node for the ng-routes.

Definition at line 225 of file BucketGraph.cpp.

225 {
226 const size_t num_nodes = nodes.size();
227
228 // Resize the neighborhoods_bitmap vector so that there's one bitmap per
229 // node.
230 neighborhoods_bitmap.resize(num_nodes);
231
232 // Pre-calculate the number of segments needed (each segment is 64 bits).
233 const size_t num_segments = (num_nodes + 63) / 64;
234
235 // Process each node to compute its neighborhood.
236 for (size_t i = 0; i < num_nodes; ++i) {
237 // Create a vector of (distance, node_index) pairs for node i.
238 std::vector<std::pair<double, size_t>> forward_distances;
239 forward_distances.reserve(num_nodes - 1);
240
241 for (size_t j = 0; j < num_nodes; ++j) {
242 if (i == j) continue;
243 double distance = getcij(i, j);
244 forward_distances.push_back({distance, j});
245 }
246
247 // Sort distances to obtain the closest nodes first.
248 pdqsort(forward_distances.begin(), forward_distances.end(),
249 [](const auto &a, const auto &b) { return a.first < b.first; });
250
251 // Initialize the neighborhood bitmap for node i with all segments set
252 // to zero.
253 neighborhoods_bitmap[i].assign(num_segments, 0ULL);
254
255 // Always include the node itself in its neighborhood.
256 size_t self_segment = i >> 6;
257 size_t self_bit = i & 63;
258 neighborhoods_bitmap[i][self_segment] |= (1ULL << self_bit);
259
260 // Add the top 'num_closest' closest nodes as forward neighbors.
261 for (size_t k = 0; k < num_closest && k < forward_distances.size(); ++k) {
262 size_t neighbor_index = forward_distances[k].second;
263 size_t segment = neighbor_index >> 6;
264 size_t bit_position = neighbor_index & 63;
265 neighborhoods_bitmap[i][segment] |= (1ULL << bit_position);
266 }
267 }
268}

◆ check_feasibility()

bool BucketGraph::check_feasibility ( const Label * fw_label,
const Label * bw_label,
const SpliceState * splice_state = nullptr )
inline

Checks the feasibility of a given forward and backward label.

This function determines if the transition from a forward label to a backward label is feasible based on resource constraints and node durations.

Definition at line 990 of file BucketGraph.h.

991 {
992 if (!fw_label || !bw_label) return false;
993
994 // Cache resources and node data.
995 const auto &fw_resources = splice_state ? splice_state->resources : fw_label->resources;
996 const auto &bw_resources = bw_label->resources;
997 const auto &node = nodes[fw_label->node_id];
998
999 const size_t n_resources = options.resources.size();
1000
1001 // Compute travel time only once.
1002 const auto travel_time = getcij(fw_label->node_id, bw_label->node_id);
1003
1004 // Assuming the "time" resource is at a known index (e.g., index 0).
1005 // If not, you can either iterate to find it once or precompute a
1006 // boolean array.
1007 if (n_resources > 0 && options.resources[0] == "time") {
1008 // Check time feasibility.
1009 const double fw_time = splice_state ? fw_resources[0] : fw_resources[0] + travel_time + node.duration;
1010 if (numericutils::gt(fw_time, bw_resources[0])) return false;
1011 } else {
1012 // Fallback: iterate to check any resource named "time".
1013 for (size_t r = 0; r < n_resources; ++r) {
1014 if (options.resources[r] == "time") {
1015 const double fw_time =
1016 splice_state ? fw_resources[r] : fw_resources[r] + travel_time + node.duration;
1017 if (numericutils::gt(fw_time, bw_resources[r])) return false;
1018 }
1019 }
1020 }
1021
1022 // Check additional resource feasibility (assuming resources[0] is
1023 // "time") and additional resources (from index 1 onward) represent
1024 // capacity constraints.
1025 for (size_t i = 1; i < n_resources; ++i) {
1026 const double fw_resource = splice_state ? fw_resources[i] : fw_resources[i] + node.demand;
1027 if (fw_resource > bw_resources[i]) return false;
1028 }
1029
1030 return true;
1031 }

89 pricing_truncated.store(true, std::memory_order_relaxed);
90 return false;
91 }
92 }
93
94 const int forward_node_id = forward_label->node_id;
95 if (backward_label == nullptr || backward_label->is_dominated || backward_label->node_id == forward_node_id) {
96 return false;
97 }
98
99 double total_cost = path_cost + backward_label->cost;
100
101#if defined(SRC)
102 if constexpr (S == Stage::Four || S == Stage::Enumerate) {
103 const double prune_limit =
104 (S == Stage::Enumerate) ? std::min(gap, enumeration_route_cutoff.load(std::memory_order_relaxed))
105 : best_cost.load(std::memory_order_relaxed);
106 if (!numericutils::lt(total_cost - max_src_splice_discount, prune_limit)) { return false; }
107
108 // In SRC stages the raw splice cost may be too pessimistic because
109 // concatenating the two partial paths can trigger additional SRC dual
110 // reductions. Apply the exact splice adjustment before the expensive
111 // bitmap/resource checks, then use the adjusted cost for safe pruning.
112 for (const auto &active_cut : active_cuts) {
113 const auto &cut = *active_cut.cut_ptr;
114 const size_t idx = active_cut.index;
115 const double dual = active_cut.dual_value;
116#if defined(SRC_MEMORY_MODE_ARC)
117 if (!cut.isSRCMemoryArc(forward_label->node_id, backward_label->node_id)) continue;
118#endif
119 const bool cut_hit = splice_state && splice_state->has_src
120 ? splice_state->SRCmap[idx] + backward_label->SRCmap[idx] >= cut.p.den
121 : forward_label->SRCmap[idx] + backward_label->SRCmap[idx] >= cut.p.den;
122 if (cut_hit) { total_cost -= dual; }
123 }
124 } else
125#endif
126 {
127 if (!numericutils::lt(total_cost, best_cost.load(std::memory_order_relaxed))) { return false; }
128 }
129
130 bool cost_acceptable;
131 if constexpr (S != Stage::Enumerate) {
132 cost_acceptable = numericutils::lt(total_cost, best_cost.load(std::memory_order_relaxed));
133 } else {
134 const double cutoff = std::min(gap, enumeration_route_cutoff.load(std::memory_order_relaxed));
135 cost_acceptable = numericutils::lt(total_cost, cutoff);
136 }
137
138 if (!cost_acceptable) { return false; }
139 if (visited_overlap(forward_label->visited_bitmap, backward_label->visited_bitmap)) { return false; }
140 if (!check_feasibility(forward_label, backward_label, splice_state)) { return false; }
141
142 scratch.candidates.push_back({backward_label, total_cost});
143 ++scratch.stats.labels_accepted;
144 if constexpr (S == Stage::Four) {
145 constexpr uint64_t kStageFourCandidateCap = static_cast<uint64_t>(N_ADD) + 2ULL;
146 const uint64_t hits = pricing_candidate_hits.fetch_add(1, std::memory_order_relaxed) + 1;
147 if (hits >= kStageFourCandidateCap) { pricing_truncated.store(true, std::memory_order_relaxed); }
148 }
149 return true;
150}

855 auto new_label = compute_red_cost(warm_labels[i], D == Direction::Forward);

858 fw_buckets[new_label->vertex].add_sorted_label(new_label);

860 bw_buckets[new_label->vertex].add_sorted_label(new_label);
861 }
862 }
863 }

878 depot_label->initialize(initial_bucket, 0.0, initial_resources, depot_id);
879 depot_label->is_extended = false;
880 depot_label->addNode(depot_id);
881 set_node_visited(depot_label->visited_bitmap, depot_id);

884 buckets[initial_bucket].node_id = depot_id;

Label * BucketGraph::compute_mono_label ( const Label * L)

◆ compute_red_cost()

Label * BucketGraph::compute_red_cost ( const WarmLabelState & L,

inline

1361 {
1362 // Calculate main and reduced costs.
1363 double real_cost = 0.0;
1364 double red_cost = 0.0;
1365
1366 // Check main resource feasibility.
1367 const double main_resource = L.resources[options.main_resources[0]];
1368 const double q_star_value = q_star[options.main_resources[0]];
1369 if (fw) {
1370 if (main_resource > q_star_value) return nullptr;
1371 } else {
1372 if (main_resource <= q_star_value) return nullptr;
1373 }
1374
1375 // Ensure sufficient nodes in the route.
1376 if (L.path_len <= 3) return nullptr;
1377
1378 // Initialize an empty SRCmap vector with one slot per pricing-active
1379 // cut. Stored cuts with tiny duals are ignored by pricing.
1380 std::vector<uint16_t> updated_SRCmap(cut_storage->activeSize(), 0);
1381
1382 // Prepare new label.
1383 auto pool = fw ? label_pool_fw : label_pool_bw;
1384 auto new_label = pool->acquire();

1386 route.clear();
1387 route.reserve(L.nodes_covered.size());

1389 new_label->path_len = L.path_len;
1390
1391 // Variables to keep track of costs.
1392 int last_node = -1;
1393
1394 // Traverse the nodes in the route (from current label to root).
1395 for (auto node_id : L.nodes_covered) {
1396 // --- NG-route Feasibility Check ---
1397 // Clear visited bitmap in the neighborhood of node_id.
1398 for (size_t seg = 0, limit = new_label->visited_bitmap.size(); seg < limit; ++seg) {
1399 uint64_t current_visited = new_label->visited_bitmap[seg];
1400 if (!current_visited) continue;
1401 uint64_t neighborhood_mask = neighborhoods_bitmap[node_id][seg];
1402 new_label->visited_bitmap[seg] = current_visited & neighborhood_mask;
1403 }
1404 set_node_visited(new_label->visited_bitmap, node_id);
1405
1406 // --- Cost Accumulation ---
1407 if (last_node != -1) {
1408 double cij_cost = getcij(last_node, node_id);
1409 real_cost += cij_cost;
1410 red_cost += cij_cost;
1411 }
1412 red_cost -= nodes[node_id].cost;
1413
1414 // --- SRC Logic ---
1415 size_t segment = node_id >> 6;
1416 const uint64_t bit_mask = bit_mask_lookup[node_id & 63];
1417 auto &cutter = cut_storage;
1418 const auto active_cuts = cutter->getActiveCuts();
1419
1420#if defined(SRC)
1421#if !defined(SRC_MEMORY_MODE_ARC)
1422 for (const auto &update : cutter->getSRCNodeUpdates(node_id)) {
1423 auto &src_val = updated_SRCmap[update.active_idx];
1424 src_val += update.add;
1425 if (src_val >= update.den) {
1426 red_cost -= update.dual;
1427 src_val -= update.den;
1428 }
1429 }
1430 for (const auto active_idx : cutter->getSRCNodeClears(node_id)) { updated_SRCmap[active_idx] = 0; }
1431#else
1432 for (const auto &active_cut : active_cuts) {
1433 const size_t idx = active_cut.index;
1434 const auto &cut = *active_cut.cut_ptr;
1435
1436 auto &src_val = updated_SRCmap[idx];
1437 if (!cut.isSRCMemoryArc(last_node, node_id)) { src_val = 0; }
1438 if (cut.isSRCset(node_id)) {
1439 src_val += cut.srcMultiplier(node_id);
1440 if (src_val >= cut.p.den) {
1441 red_cost -= active_cut.dual_value;
1442 src_val -= cut.p.den;
1443 }
1444 }
1445 }
1446#endif
1447#endif
1448
1449 // --- Arc and Branching Dual Adjustments ---
1450#if defined(RCC) || defined(EXACT_RCC)
1451 if (last_node != -1) { red_cost -= arc_duals.getDual(last_node, node_id); }
1452#endif
1453 if (branching_duals->size() > 0 && last_node != -1) {
1454 red_cost -= branching_duals->getDual(last_node, node_id);
1455 }
1456
1457 last_node = node_id; // Update last_node.
1458 }
1459
1460 // Set final label properties.
1461 new_label->cost = red_cost;
1462 new_label->real_cost = real_cost;
1463 new_label->parent = nullptr;
1464 new_label->node_id = L.node_id;
1465 new_label->is_extended = false;
1466 new_label->resources = L.resources;
1467 new_label->SRCmap = updated_SRCmap;
1468 new_label->fresh = false;
1469
1470 // Determine bucket number for the new label.
1471 std::vector<double> new_resources(options.resources.size());
1472 for (size_t i = 0; i < options.resources.size(); ++i) { new_resources[i] = new_label->resources[i]; }
1473 int bucket = fw ? get_bucket_number<Direction::Forward>(new_label->node_id, new_resources)
1474 : get_bucket_number<Direction::Backward>(new_label->node_id, new_resources);
1475 new_label->vertex = bucket;
1476
1477 return new_label;
1478 }

bool fw )

Computes the phi values for a given bucket ID and direction.

127 {
128 std::vector<int> phi;
129
130 // Get references based on the direction.
131 auto &buckets = fw ? fw_buckets : bw_buckets;
132
133 // Multi-dimensional resource case.
134 if (options.resources.size() > 1) {
135 if (bucket_id < 0 || bucket_id >= static_cast<int>(buckets.size())) return phi;
136
137 const int n_dims = static_cast<int>(intervals.size());
138 std::vector<double> base_intervals(n_dims, 0.0);
139 std::vector<int> splits_per_dim(n_dims, 1);
140
141 // Get the node associated with the current bucket.
142 int node_id = buckets[bucket_id].node_id;
143 const VRPNode &node = nodes[node_id];
144
145 // Recompute splits and base intervals per dimension using the same
146 // logic as in define_buckets.
147 for (int r = 0; r < n_dims; ++r) {
148 double full_range = R_max[r] - R_min[r];
149 double node_range = node.ub[r] - node.lb[r];
150 int splits = 1;
151 if (std::fabs(full_range) > std::numeric_limits<double>::epsilon()) {
152 double splits_d = (node_range * intervals[r].interval) / full_range;
153 splits = std::max(1, static_cast<int>(std::round(splits_d)));
154 }
155 splits_per_dim[r] = splits;
156 base_intervals[r] = node_range / static_cast<double>(splits);
157 }
158
159 auto &num_buckets_index = fw ? num_buckets_index_fw : num_buckets_index_bw;
160 int local_bucket = bucket_id - num_buckets_index[node_id];
161
162 // Reconstruct the local multi-dimensional position vector for the
163 // current bucket using the same mixed-radix scheme as get_bucket_number.
164 std::vector<int> pos(n_dims, 0);
165 int rem = local_bucket;
166 for (int r = 0; r < n_dims; ++r) {
167 const int splits = splits_per_dim[r];
168 pos[r] = rem % splits;
169 rem /= splits;
170 }
171
172 // Build Phi in the paper's kappa order. Forward buckets have
173 // lb = l_v + kappa * d; backward buckets have ub = u_v - kappa * d.
174 // In both senses Phi_b contains adjacent component-wise smaller
175 // buckets, i.e. kappa' = kappa - e_r. For backward this is a higher
176 // physical resource interval, but still a smaller kappa.
177 for (int r = 0; r < n_dims; ++r) {
178 if (pos[r] == 0) continue;
179 std::vector<int> neighbor_pos = pos;
180 --neighbor_pos[r];
181
182 int candidate_bucket = num_buckets_index[node_id];
183 int multiplier = 1;
184 for (int s = 0; s < n_dims; ++s) {
185 const int splits = splits_per_dim[s];
186 candidate_bucket += neighbor_pos[s] * multiplier;
187 multiplier *= splits;
188 }
189
190 if (candidate_bucket >= 0 && candidate_bucket < static_cast<int>(buckets.size()) &&
191 buckets[candidate_bucket].node_id == node_id) {
192#ifdef FIX_BUCKETS
193 bool not_fixed = fw ? is_bucket_not_fixed_forward(bucket_id, candidate_bucket)
194 : is_bucket_not_fixed_backward(bucket_id, candidate_bucket);
195 // if (not_fixed)
196#endif
197 { phi.push_back(candidate_bucket); }
198 }
199 }
200 }
201 // Single-resource case (simpler).
202 else {
203 // Phi is kappa - 1 for both senses. Because backward buckets are stored
204 // in paper kappa order (kappa 0 is the interval touching u_v), this is
205 // also bucket_id - 1 even though physical resource values go up.
206 int neighbor = bucket_id - 1;
207 bool valid_neighbor = neighbor >= 0;
208 if (valid_neighbor && neighbor >= 0 && neighbor < static_cast<int>(buckets.size()) &&
209 buckets[neighbor].node_id == buckets[bucket_id].node_id) {
210#ifdef FIX_BUCKETS
211 bool not_fixed = fw ? is_bucket_not_fixed_forward(neighbor, bucket_id)
212 : is_bucket_not_fixed_backward(neighbor, bucket_id);
213 // if (not_fixed)
214#endif
215 { phi.push_back(neighbor); }
216 }
217 }
218 return phi;
219}

225 const auto label_count = labels.size() + extra_labels.size();
226 if (label_count == 0) continue;
227 scratch.prepare_candidates(label_count);
228
229 for (const Label *L_bw : labels) {

232 }

235 }
236 for (const Label *L_bw : extra_labels) {

242 }

122 const size_t n_chunks = (fw_buckets_size + chunk_size - 1) / chunk_size;
123
124 auto bulk_sender = stdexec::bulk(
125 stdexec::just(), n_chunks,
126 [this, chunk_size, &pass](std::size_t chunk_idx) {
127 const size_t start_bucket = chunk_idx * chunk_size;
128 const size_t end_bucket = std::min(start_bucket + chunk_size, static_cast<size_t>(fw_buckets_size));
129
130 for (size_t bucket = start_bucket; bucket < end_bucket; ++bucket) {
131 if constexpr (S == Stage::Four) {
132 if (pricing_truncated.load(std::memory_order_relaxed)) break;
133 }
134 const auto &bucket_labels = fw_buckets[bucket].get_labels();
135 for (const Label *L : bucket_labels) {
136 if constexpr (S == Stage::Four) {
137 if (pricing_truncated.load(std::memory_order_relaxed)) break;
138 }
139 if (L->is_dominated) continue;
140
141 pass.non_dominated_labels.fetch_add(1, std::memory_order_relaxed);
142
143 const auto &to_arcs = fw_buckets[bucket].template get_bucket_arcs<Direction::Forward>();
144 for (const auto &arc : to_arcs) {
145 if constexpr (S == Stage::Four) {
146 if (pricing_truncated.load(std::memory_order_relaxed)) break;
147 }
148 concatenate_from_forward_arc<S, SYM>(L, arc, pass);
149 }
150 }
151 }
152 });
153
154 auto work = stdexec::starts_on(merge_sched, bulk_sender);
155 stdexec::sync_wait(std::move(work));
156 last_concatenation_labels_tested = concatenation_labels_tested.load(std::memory_order_relaxed);
157 last_concatenation_labels_accepted = concatenation_labels_accepted.load(std::memory_order_relaxed);

◆ considerRegenerate()

bool BucketGraph::considerRegenerate ( )
inline

Definition at line 1134 of file BucketGraph.h.

1134 {
1135 constexpr double REGEN_RATIO_THRESHOLD = 800.0; // RouteOpt's value.
1136 constexpr int REGEN_BUCKETS_CAP = 200000; // ~10k arcs/vertex
1137 // analog for our scale.
1138
1139 // Sample the most recent labeling pass: total dominance scans across
1140 // all forward buckets, divided by non-dominant label count summed over
1141 // both directions. Avoids div-by-zero when the pass produced nothing.
1142 double total_checks = 0.0;
1143 for (int v : dominance_checks_per_bucket) total_checks += v;
1144 const int non_dom_total = non_dominated_labels_per_bucket + non_dominated_labels_per_bucket_bw;
1145 if (non_dom_total <= 0) return false;
1146 const double pass_ratio = total_checks / static_cast<double>(non_dom_total);
1147
1148 // Update running average.
1149 regen_ratio_sum += pass_ratio;
1150 ++regen_ratio_count;
1151 const double avg_ratio = regen_ratio_sum / regen_ratio_count;
1152
1153 if (avg_ratio <= REGEN_RATIO_THRESHOLD) return false;
1154
1155 // Guards.
1156 const int total_buckets = static_cast<int>(fw_buckets.size() + bw_buckets.size());
1157 if (total_buckets >= REGEN_BUCKETS_CAP) return false;
1158
1159 if (bucket_interval > std::numeric_limits<int>::max() / 2) return false;
1160 const int new_interval = bucket_interval * 2;
1161 print_info("Refining bucket_interval {} -> {} (avg dom-check ratio {:.1f})\n", bucket_interval, new_interval,
1162 avg_ratio);
1163 bucket_interval = new_interval;
1164 redefine(bucket_interval);
1165
1166 // Reset the running average so we judge the new partition on its own.
1167 regen_ratio_sum = 0.0;
1168 regen_ratio_count = 0;
1169 fixed = false;
1170 return true;
1171 }

◆ define_buckets() [1/2]

template<Direction D>
void BucketGraph::define_buckets ( )

Defines the buckets for the BucketGraph.

This function determines the number of buckets based on the time intervals and assigns buckets to the graph. It computes resource bounds for each vertex and defines the bounds of each bucket.

150 // 1. Save the old buckets and fixed status.
151 std::vector<Bucket> old_buckets;
152 std::vector<bool> was_fixed;
153 auto &buckets = assign_buckets<D>(fw_buckets, bw_buckets);
154 size_t old_size = buckets.size();
155
156 // Map to store the fixed range (min_lb and max_ub) for each node pair.
157 ankerl::unordered_dense::map<int,
158 ankerl::unordered_dense::map<int, std::pair<std::vector<double>, std::vector<double>>>>
159 node_pair_fixed_ranges;
160
161 if (old_size > 0) {
162 old_buckets = buckets;
163 was_fixed.resize(old_size * old_size, false);
164 auto &old_bitmap = assign_buckets<D>(fw_fixed_buckets_bitmap, bw_fixed_buckets_bitmap);
165
166 // Mark fixed bucket pairs.
167 for (size_t i = 0; i < old_size; ++i) {
168 for (size_t j = 0; j < old_size; ++j) {
169 size_t pos = i * old_size + j;
170 if (pos / 64 < old_bitmap.size()) {
171 was_fixed[pos] = ((old_bitmap[pos / 64] & (1ULL << (pos % 64))) != 0);
172 }
173 }
174 }
175
176 const int num_intervals = options.main_resources.size();
177 // For each fixed bucket pair, update the fixed range for the
178 // corresponding node pair.
179 for (size_t i = 0; i < old_size; ++i) {
180 for (size_t j = 0; j < old_size; ++j) {
181 if (!was_fixed[i * old_size + j]) continue;
182 const auto &bucket_from = old_buckets[i];
183 const auto &bucket_to = old_buckets[j];
184 int from_node = bucket_from.node_id;
185 int to_node = bucket_to.node_id;
186 // Initialize if necessary.
187 if (node_pair_fixed_ranges[from_node].find(to_node) == node_pair_fixed_ranges[from_node].end()) {
188 node_pair_fixed_ranges[from_node][to_node] = {
189 std::vector<double>(num_intervals, std::numeric_limits<double>::max()),
190 std::vector<double>(num_intervals, std::numeric_limits<double>::lowest())};
191 }
192 auto &[min_lbs, max_ubs] = node_pair_fixed_ranges[from_node][to_node];
193 for (int r = 0; r < num_intervals; ++r) {
194 min_lbs[r] = std::min(min_lbs[r], bucket_from.lb[r]);
195 max_ubs[r] = std::max(max_ubs[r], bucket_from.ub[r]);
196 }
197 }
198 }
199 } // End if old buckets exist.
200
201 // 2. Clear the current buckets and fixed bitmap.
202 buckets.clear();
203 auto &fixed_buckets_bitmap = assign_buckets<D>(fw_fixed_buckets_bitmap, bw_fixed_buckets_bitmap);
204 fixed_buckets_bitmap.clear();
205
206 // 3. Prepare variables and containers for new bucket definition.
207 const int num_intervals = options.main_resources.size();
208 std::vector<double> total_ranges(num_intervals); // unused in current code
209 std::vector<double> base_intervals(num_intervals); // unused in current code
210
211 auto &num_buckets = assign_buckets<D>(num_buckets_fw, num_buckets_bw);
212 auto &num_buckets_index = assign_buckets<D>(num_buckets_index_fw, num_buckets_index_bw);
213 auto &node_interval_trees = assign_buckets<D>(fw_node_interval_trees, bw_node_interval_trees);
214 auto &buckets_size = assign_buckets<D>(fw_buckets_size, bw_buckets_size);
215 auto &bucket_splits = assign_buckets<D>(fw_bucket_splits, bw_bucket_splits);
216
217 const size_t num_nodes = nodes.size();
218 num_buckets.resize(num_nodes);
219 num_buckets_index.resize(num_nodes);
220 node_interval_trees.assign(num_nodes, SplayTree());
221
222 // Lambda to calculate an interval for one dimension. Backward buckets are
223 // deliberately emitted in the paper's kappa order:
224 // kappa = 0 -> interval ending at u_v,
225 // kappa = 1 -> next lower interval, ...
226 // This keeps component-wise smaller buckets equal to smaller local bucket
227 // positions in both forward and backward arrays.
228 auto calculate_interval = [&](double lb, double ub, double base_interval, int pos, int max_intervals,
229 bool is_forward) -> std::pair<double, double> {
230 double start, end;
231 if (is_forward) {
232 start = (pos == 0) ? lb : lb + pos * base_interval;
233 end = (pos == max_intervals - 1) ? ub : lb + (pos + 1) * base_interval;
234 } else {
235 start = (pos == max_intervals - 1) ? lb : ub - (pos + 1) * base_interval;
236 end = (pos == 0) ? ub : ub - pos * base_interval;
237 }
238 start = roundToTwoDecimalPlaces(start);
239 end = roundToTwoDecimalPlaces(end);
240 return {start, end};
241 };
242
243 int cum_sum = 0;
244 int bucket_index = 0;
245 std::vector<double> interval_start(num_intervals);
246 std::vector<double> interval_end(num_intervals);
247 std::vector<int> pos(num_intervals,
248 0); // combination indices for multi-dimensions
249
250 // 4. Process each node to define new buckets.
251 for (const auto &vrp_node : nodes) {
252 std::vector<int> node_split_counts(num_intervals);
253 std::vector<double> node_base_interval(num_intervals);
254 // Determine the number of splits and base interval for each
255 // resource dimension.
256 for (int r = 0; r < num_intervals; ++r) {
257 double full_range = R_max[r] - R_min[r];
258 double node_range = vrp_node.ub[r] - vrp_node.lb[r];
259 int splits = 1;
260 if (std::fabs(full_range) > std::numeric_limits<double>::epsilon()) {
261 double splits_d = (node_range * intervals[r].interval) / full_range;
262 splits = std::max(1, static_cast<int>(std::round(splits_d)));
263 }
264 node_split_counts[r] = splits;
265 node_base_interval[r] = (vrp_node.ub[r] - vrp_node.lb[r]) / static_cast<double>(splits);
266 }
267 // Optionally store node-specific split counts.
268 bucket_splits[vrp_node.id] = node_split_counts;
269
270 int n_buckets = 0;
271 if (num_intervals == 1) {
272 // Single-dimensional case.
273 for (int j = 0; j < node_split_counts[0]; ++j) {
274 auto [int_start, int_end] = calculate_interval(vrp_node.lb[0], vrp_node.ub[0], node_base_interval[0], j,
275 node_split_counts[0], D == Direction::Forward);
276 if constexpr (D == Direction::Backward)
277 int_start = std::max(int_start, vrp_node.lb[0]);
278 else
279 int_end = std::min(int_end, vrp_node.ub[0]);
280 buckets.push_back(Bucket(vrp_node.id, std::vector<double>{int_start}, std::vector<double>{int_end}));
281 n_buckets++;
282 cum_sum++;
283 }
284 } else {
285 // Multi-dimensional: iterate over all combinations.
286 fmt::print("Multiple intervals\n");
287 std::fill(pos.begin(), pos.end(), 0);
288 while (true) {
289 for (int r = 0; r < num_intervals; ++r) {
290 auto [int_start, int_end] =
291 calculate_interval(vrp_node.lb[r], vrp_node.ub[r], node_base_interval[r], pos[r],
292 node_split_counts[r], D == Direction::Forward);
293 interval_start[r] = int_start;
294 interval_end[r] = int_end;
295 if constexpr (D == Direction::Backward)
296 interval_start[r] = std::max(interval_start[r], R_min[r]);
297 else
298 interval_end[r] = std::min(interval_end[r], R_max[r]);
299 }
300 buckets.push_back(Bucket(vrp_node.id, interval_start, interval_end));
301 n_buckets++;
302 cum_sum++;
303
304 // Generate next combination.
305 int i = 0;
306 while (i < num_intervals) {
307 ++pos[i];
308 if (pos[i] < node_split_counts[i])
309 break;
310 else {
311 pos[i] = 0;
312 ++i;
313 }
314 }
315 if (i == num_intervals) break;
316 }
317 }
318 num_buckets[vrp_node.id] = n_buckets;
319 num_buckets_index[vrp_node.id] = cum_sum - n_buckets;
320 // Optionally assign node_interval_trees[vrp_node.id] = node_tree;
321 //
322 if constexpr (D == Direction::Forward) { n_segments = (n_buckets + 63) >> 6; }
323 }
324
325 // 5. Precompute RC2 bucket metadata for whole-bucket prune.
326 // Forward dominance needs candidate rc2 <= label rc2, so keep a prefix
327 // minimum of bucket lower bounds. Backward buckets are reversed, so
328 // candidate rc2 >= label rc2 also lives in the prefix.
329 auto &rc2_bin = assign_buckets<D>(fw_rc2_bin, bw_rc2_bin);
330 auto &rc2_till_this_bin = assign_buckets<D>(fw_rc2_till_this_bin, bw_rc2_till_this_bin);
331 rc2_bin.assign(num_nodes, {});
332 rc2_till_this_bin.assign(num_nodes, {});
333 for (int node_id = 0; node_id < static_cast<int>(num_nodes); ++node_id) {
334 int count = num_buckets[node_id];
335 rc2_bin[node_id].assign(count, 0.0);
336 rc2_till_this_bin[node_id].assign(count, 0.0);
337 if (count == 0) continue;
338
339 for (int local_b = 0; local_b < count; ++local_b) {
340 int global_b = num_buckets_index[node_id] + local_b;
341 const auto &bucket = buckets[global_b];
342 double rc2_value = 0.0;
343 if constexpr (D == Direction::Forward) {
344 if (bucket.lb.size() > 1) { rc2_value = bucket.lb[1]; }
345 } else {
346 if (bucket.ub.size() > 1) { rc2_value = bucket.ub[1]; }
347 }
348 rc2_bin[node_id][local_b] = rc2_value;
349 if (local_b == 0) {
350 rc2_till_this_bin[node_id][local_b] = rc2_value;
351 } else {
352 if constexpr (D == Direction::Forward) {
353 rc2_till_this_bin[node_id][local_b] = std::min(rc2_till_this_bin[node_id][local_b - 1], rc2_value);
354 } else {
355 rc2_till_this_bin[node_id][local_b] = std::max(rc2_till_this_bin[node_id][local_b - 1], rc2_value);
356 }
357 }
358 }
359 }
360
361 // 6. Process fixed bucket ranges (if available).
362 if (!node_pair_fixed_ranges.empty()) {
363 size_t new_size = buckets.size();
364 size_t required_bitmap_words = std::max(size_t(1), ((new_size * new_size) + 63) / 64);
365 fixed_buckets_bitmap.clear();
366 fixed_buckets_bitmap.resize(required_bitmap_words, 0);
367 for (size_t from = 0; from < new_size; ++from) {
368 const auto &bucket_from = buckets[from];
369 int from_node = bucket_from.node_id;
370 if (node_pair_fixed_ranges.find(from_node) == node_pair_fixed_ranges.end()) continue;
371 for (size_t to = 0; to < new_size; ++to) {
372 const auto &bucket_to = buckets[to];
373 int to_node = bucket_to.node_id;
374 if (node_pair_fixed_ranges[from_node].find(to_node) == node_pair_fixed_ranges[from_node].end())
375 continue;
376 const auto &range = node_pair_fixed_ranges[from_node][to_node];
377 bool in_range = true;
378 for (int r = 0; r < options.main_resources.size(); ++r) {
379 if (bucket_from.lb[r] < range.first[r] || bucket_from.ub[r] > range.second[r]) {
380 in_range = false;
381 break;
382 }
383 }
384 if (in_range) {
385 size_t bit_pos = from * new_size + to;
386 size_t word_idx = bit_pos / 64;
387 if (word_idx < fixed_buckets_bitmap.size()) {
388 fixed_buckets_bitmap[word_idx] |= (1ULL << (bit_pos % 64));
389 }
390 }
391 }
392 }
393 }
394
395 // 6. Finalize: record the total bucket count.
396 buckets_size = buckets.size();
397}

1635 if (product < fixed_num_buckets) {
1636 int factor = fixed_num_buckets / product;

1638 }
1639 }
1640
1641 // Compute the width (base interval) for each resource
1642 // dimension.
1643 std::vector<double> node_base_interval(num_intervals);

1678 pos[r], node_split_counts[r], D == Direction::Forward);

1680 interval_end[r] = end;

1684 } else {

1687 }

◆ DominatedInCompWiseSmallerBuckets() [1/2]

template<Direction D, Stage S>
bool BucketGraph::DominatedInCompWiseSmallerBuckets ( const Label *__restrict__ L,
int bucket,
const std::vector< double > &__restrict__ c_bar,
std::vector< uint64_t > &__restrict__ Bvisited,
std::vector< uint32_t > & touched_segments,
uint & stat_n_dom )
inlinenoexcept

Determines if a label is dominated in component-wise smaller buckets.

This function checks if a given label is dominated by any other label in component-wise smaller buckets. The dominance is determined based on the cost and order of the buckets.

Definition at line 959 of file BucketSolve.h.

963 {
964 // Cache direction-specific data
965 const auto &buckets = assign_buckets<D>(fw_buckets, bw_buckets);
966 const auto &Phi = assign_buckets<D>(Phi_fw, Phi_bw);
967 const auto &bucket_scc_rank = assign_buckets<D>(fw_bucket_scc_rank, bw_bucket_scc_rank);
968 const auto &num_buckets_index = assign_buckets<D>(num_buckets_index_fw, num_buckets_index_bw);
969 const auto &num_buckets = assign_buckets<D>(num_buckets_fw, num_buckets_bw);
970 const auto &rc2_bin = assign_buckets<D>(fw_rc2_bin, bw_rc2_bin);
971 const auto &rc2_till_this_bin = assign_buckets<D>(fw_rc2_till_this_bin, bw_rc2_till_this_bin);
972 const bool profile_labeling = options.profile_labeling;
973
974 // Cache frequently used label properties
975 const int b_L = L->vertex;
976 const double label_cost = L->cost;
977 const int label_rank = bucket_scc_rank[b_L];
978 const auto &label_res = L->resources;
979 const size_t n_res = options.resources.size();
980
981 // Use static thread-local stack to avoid repeated allocations
982 static thread_local std::vector<int> stack_buffer;
983 if (stack_buffer.capacity() < R_SIZE) { stack_buffer.reserve(R_SIZE); }
984 stack_buffer.clear();
985 stack_buffer.push_back(bucket);
986
987 // Direct pointer to bit mask lookup for fast bit operations
988 const uint64_t *const bit_mask_lookup_ptr = bit_mask_lookup.data();
989
990 // Read-only dominance scan: ask only "does some predecessor label cur
991 // dominate L?". Per Sadykov & Vanderbeck (2021), this routine MUST NOT
992 // mutate labels in compwise-smaller buckets — they belong to already-
993 // processed (or concurrently processed) buckets, and marking them
994 // dominated mid-walk silently drops queued pending labels. Cost is a
995 // necessary condition for cur-dominates-L, so once cur->cost > L.cost
996 // (sorted bin) we can break.
997 const double cost_hi_break = label_cost + numericutils::eps;
998#if defined(SRC)
999 constexpr bool src_adjusted_dominance = (S == Stage::Four || S == Stage::Enumerate);
1000#else
1001 constexpr bool src_adjusted_dominance = false;
1002#endif
1003
1004 auto inline_check_dominance = [&](const BucketLabelSoAView &view, uint &n_dom) -> bool {
1005 const size_t size = view.labels.size();
1006 if (profile_labeling) profile_record_inner_bin_scan(D, S, size);
1007 for (size_t i = 0; i < size; ++i) {
1008 // Bin is RC-sorted ascending; once cur is more expensive than L,
1009 // no later label can dominate L (cost ordering).
1010 if constexpr (!src_adjusted_dominance) {
1011 if (view.costs[i] > cost_hi_break) break;
1012 }
1013
1014 bool resource_candidate = true;
1015 if constexpr (D == Direction::Forward) {
1016 for (size_t r = 1; r < n_res; ++r) {
1017 if (numericutils::gt(view.resources[r][i], label_res[r])) {
1018 resource_candidate = false;
1019 break;
1020 }
1021 }
1022 if (resource_candidate && n_res > 0 && numericutils::gt(view.resources[0][i], label_res[0])) {
1023 resource_candidate = false;
1024 }
1025 } else {
1026 for (size_t r = 1; r < n_res; ++r) {
1027 if (numericutils::lt(view.resources[r][i], label_res[r])) {
1028 resource_candidate = false;
1029 break;
1030 }
1031 }
1032 if (resource_candidate && n_res > 0 && numericutils::lt(view.resources[0][i], label_res[0])) {
1033 resource_candidate = false;
1034 }
1035 }
1036 if (!resource_candidate) continue;
1037
1038 Label *cur = view.labels[i];
1039 if (cur->is_dominated) continue;
1040 if (is_dominated<D, S>(L, cur)) {
1041 ++n_dom;
1042 return true;
1043 }
1044 }
1045 return false;
1046 };
1047
1048 auto inline_check_extra_dominance = [&](std::span<Label *const> labels, uint &n_dom) -> bool {
1049 if (profile_labeling) profile_record_inner_bin_scan(D, S, labels.size());
1050 for (Label *label : labels) {
1051 if (label->is_dominated) continue;
1052 // extra is unsorted -> no break, just skip.
1053 if constexpr (!src_adjusted_dominance) {
1054 if (label->cost > cost_hi_break) continue;
1055 }
1056 if (is_dominated<D, S>(L, label)) {
1057 ++n_dom;
1058 return true;
1059 }
1060 }
1061 return false;
1062 };
1063
1064 // Main DFS traversal loop
1065 while (!stack_buffer.empty()) {
1066 const int current_bucket = stack_buffer.back();
1067 stack_buffer.pop_back();
1068
1069 const size_t segment = static_cast<size_t>(current_bucket) >> 6;
1070 const uint64_t bit = bit_mask_lookup_ptr[current_bucket & 63];
1071
1072 if (unlikely(Bvisited[segment] & bit)) continue;
1073
1074 // Mark as visited
1075 if (Bvisited[segment] == 0) { touched_segments.push_back(static_cast<uint32_t>(segment)); }
1076 Bvisited[segment] |= bit;
1077
1078 // Fast path: if label cost is lower and bucket precedes L in the SCC
1079 // order, return early
1080 if (likely(label_cost < c_bar[current_bucket] && bucket_scc_rank[current_bucket] < label_rank)) return false;
1081
1082 // Whole-bucket prune: if no label in this bucket or any predecessor bin
1083 // at this node can dominate L in the second resource, skip it entirely.
1084 if (options.resources.size() > 1) {
1085 const auto &bucket = buckets[current_bucket];
1086 const int node_id = bucket.node_id;
1087 const int local_bucket = current_bucket - num_buckets_index[node_id];
1088 if (local_bucket >= 0 && local_bucket < num_buckets[node_id]) {
1089 const double label_rc2 = L->resources[1];
1090 if constexpr (D == Direction::Forward) {
1091 if (numericutils::gt(rc2_till_this_bin[node_id][local_bucket], label_rc2)) { continue; }
1092 } else {
1093 if (numericutils::lt(rc2_till_this_bin[node_id][local_bucket], label_rc2)) { continue; }
1094 }
1095 }
1096 }
1097
1098 // Skip dominance check for L's own bucket
1099 if (b_L != current_bucket) {
1100 const auto &mother_bucket = buckets[current_bucket];
1101 if (mother_bucket.check_dominance_soa(L, inline_check_dominance, inline_check_extra_dominance, stat_n_dom))
1102 return true;
1103 }
1104
1105 // Process neighbor buckets using pointer arithmetic and prefetching
1106 const auto &neighbors = Phi[current_bucket];
1107 const size_t n_neighbors = neighbors.size();
1108 const int *neighbor_ptr = neighbors.data();
1109 const int *neighbor_end = neighbor_ptr + n_neighbors;
1110 for (; neighbor_ptr != neighbor_end; ++neighbor_ptr) {
1111 const int b_prime = *neighbor_ptr;
1112 const auto &bucket = buckets[b_prime];
1113 const int node_id = bucket.node_id;
1114 const int local_b = b_prime - num_buckets_index[node_id];
1115 if (options.resources.size() > 1 && local_b >= 0 && local_b < num_buckets[node_id]) {
1116 const double label_rc2 = L->resources[1];
1117 if constexpr (D == Direction::Forward) {
1118 if (numericutils::gt(rc2_till_this_bin[node_id][local_b], label_rc2)) { continue; }
1119 } else {
1120 if (numericutils::lt(rc2_till_this_bin[node_id][local_b], label_rc2)) { continue; }
1121 }
1122 }
1123
1124 const size_t word_idx = static_cast<size_t>(b_prime) >> 6;
1125 const uint64_t mask = bit_mask_lookup_ptr[b_prime & 63];
1126 __builtin_prefetch(&Bvisited[word_idx], 0,
1127 1); // Prefetch Bvisited for this segment
1128 if (!(Bvisited[word_idx] & mask)) stack_buffer.push_back(b_prime);
1129 }
1130 }
1131
1132 return false;
1133}

bool BucketGraph::dominates_resource_path ( const Label *__restrict new_label,

inlinenoexcept

Checks if a label is dominated by a new label based on cost and resource conditions.

841 {
842 // Compare resource vectors.
843 const auto *__restrict new_res = new_label->resources.data();
844 const auto *__restrict lbl_res = label->resources.data();
845
846 // Prefetch resource data
847 __builtin_prefetch(new_res, 0,
848 3); // 0 = read only, 3 = high temporal locality
849 __builtin_prefetch(lbl_res, 0, 3);
850
851 const size_t n_res = options.resources.size();
852 if constexpr (D == Direction::Forward) {
853 // In the Forward direction, each resource value of 'label' must
854 // not exceed that of 'new_label'. Check resource 0 last because bucket
855 // traversal already gives it partial ordering.
856 for (size_t i = 1; i < n_res; ++i) {
857 if (numericutils::gt(lbl_res[i], new_res[i])) { return false; }
858 }
859 if (n_res > 0 && numericutils::gt(lbl_res[0], new_res[0])) { return false; }
860 } else if constexpr (D == Direction::Backward) {
861 // In the Backward direction, each resource value of 'label'
862 // must not be less than that of 'new_label'. Check resource 0 last.
863 for (size_t i = 1; i < n_res; ++i) {
864 if (numericutils::lt(lbl_res[i], new_res[i])) { return false; }
865 }
866 if (n_res > 0 && numericutils::lt(lbl_res[0], new_res[0])) { return false; }
867 }
868
869 // visits of label.
870 if constexpr (S == Stage::Three || S == Stage::Four || S == Stage::Enumerate) {
871 const size_t n_bitmap = label->visited_bitmap.size();
872
873 // Prefetch bitmap data
874 __builtin_prefetch(label->visited_bitmap.data(), 0, 3);
875 __builtin_prefetch(new_label->visited_bitmap.data(), 0, 3);
876
877 for (size_t i = 0; i < n_bitmap; ++i) {
878 // Every visited node in 'label' must also be visited in
879 // 'new_label'.
880 if ((label->visited_bitmap[i] & new_label->visited_bitmap[i]) != label->visited_bitmap[i]) { return false; }
881 }
882 }
883 return true;
884}

567 // Pre-allocated result vector to avoid allocations for the common case of
568 // 0-1 results
569 static thread_local std::vector<Label *> result_vector;
570 Label *result = nullptr;
571
572 // Prepare temporary resource storage (resize to current resources size)
573 static thread_local std::vector<double> new_resources;
574 const size_t n_resources = options.resources.size();
575 new_resources.resize(n_resources);
576
577 // Cache initial values from L_prime
578 const int initial_node_id = L_prime->node_id;
579 const auto &initial_resources = L_prime->resources; // Use reference instead of copy
580 const double initial_cost = L_prime->cost;
581
582 // Determine target node ID based on arc type. A bucket jump arc is stored
583 // as a marked BucketArc whose to_bucket is the jump bucket and whose
584 // jump_to_node is the head of the original arc.
585 int node_id;
586 int jump_bucket_id = -1;
587 bool is_jump_arc = false;
588 if constexpr (A == ArcType::Bucket) {
589 if (gamma.jump) {
590 if (gamma.jump_to_node < 0) {
591 if constexpr (F == Full::Reverse)
592 return -1;
593 else
594 return result;
595 }
596 node_id = gamma.jump_to_node;
597 jump_bucket_id = gamma.to_bucket;
598 is_jump_arc = true;
599 } else {
600 node_id = assign_buckets<D>(fw_buckets, bw_buckets)[gamma.to_bucket].node_id;
601 }
602 } else if constexpr (A == ArcType::Node) {
603 node_id = gamma.to;
604 } else { // Jump arc
605 auto &buckets = assign_buckets<D>(fw_buckets, bw_buckets);
606 node_id = gamma.to_job;
607 jump_bucket_id = gamma.jump_bucket;
608 is_jump_arc = true;
609 if (node_id < 0 || jump_bucket_id < 0 || jump_bucket_id >= static_cast<int>(buckets.size())) {
610 if constexpr (F == Full::Reverse)
611 return -1;
612 else
613 return result;
614 }
615 }
616
617 // Early rejection: if we loop back or the node was already visited
618 if (node_id == initial_node_id || is_node_visited(L_prime->visited_bitmap, node_id)) {
619 if constexpr (F == Full::Reverse)
620 return -1;
621 else
622 return result;
623 }
624
625 // For stages where fixed arcs matter
626 if constexpr (S >= Stage::Three || S == Stage::Eliminate) {
627 if ((D == Direction::Forward && is_arc_fixed(initial_node_id, node_id)) ||
628 (D == Direction::Backward && is_arc_fixed(node_id, initial_node_id))) {
629 if constexpr (F == Full::Reverse)
630 return -1;
631 else
632 return result;
633 }
634 }
635
636 // Process resources (feasibility check)
637 if constexpr (F != Full::TSP) {
638 auto process_jump_resources = [&]() -> bool {
639 auto &buckets = assign_buckets<D>(fw_buckets, bw_buckets);
640 if (jump_bucket_id < 0 || jump_bucket_id >= static_cast<int>(buckets.size())) return false;
641 const auto &jump_bucket = buckets[jump_bucket_id];
642
643 std::array<double, R_SIZE> jumped_resources = initial_resources;
644 for (size_t i = 0; i < n_resources; ++i) {
645 if constexpr (D == Direction::Forward) {
646 jumped_resources[i] = std::max(jumped_resources[i], static_cast<double>(jump_bucket.lb[i]));
647 } else {
648 jumped_resources[i] = std::min(jumped_resources[i], static_cast<double>(jump_bucket.ub[i]));
649 }
650 }
651 return process_all_resources<D>(new_resources, jumped_resources, gamma, nodes[node_id], n_resources);
652 };
653
654 if constexpr (A != ArcType::Jump) {
655 // Only copy resources for non-jump arcs (jump arcs already copied)
656 std::memcpy(new_resources.data(), initial_resources.data(), n_resources * sizeof(double));
657
658 const bool feasible = is_jump_arc ? process_jump_resources()
659 : process_all_resources<D>(new_resources, initial_resources, gamma,
660 nodes[node_id], n_resources);
661 if (!feasible) {
662 if constexpr (F == Full::Reverse)
663 return -1;
664 else
665 return result;
666 }
667 } else {
668 if (!process_jump_resources()) {
669 if constexpr (F == Full::Reverse)
670 return -1;
671 else
672 return result;
673 }
674 }
675 }
676
677 // For PSTEP/TSP: Check path length constraints
678 if constexpr (F == Full::PSTEP || F == Full::TSP) {
679 const int next_path_len = L_prime->path_len + 1;
680 if (next_path_len > options.max_path_size ||
681 (next_path_len == options.max_path_size && node_id != options.end_depot)) {
682 if constexpr (F == Full::Reverse)
683 return -1;
684 else
685 return result;
686 }
687 }
688
689 // Get bucket number for the new label
690 int to_bucket = -1;
691 if constexpr (A == ArcType::Bucket) {
692 to_bucket = get_bucket_number<D>(node_id, new_resources);
693 } else if constexpr (A == ArcType::Jump) {
694 to_bucket = get_bucket_number<D>(node_id, new_resources);
695 } else if constexpr (A == ArcType::Node) {
696 to_bucket = get_bucket_number<D>(node_id, new_resources);
697 }
698
699 // Handle Reverse mode early
700 if constexpr (F == Full::Reverse) {
701 if (new_resources[options.main_resources[0]] <= std::floor(q_star[options.main_resources[0]])) return -1;
702
703 return to_bucket;
704 }
705
706 // Calculate new cost
707#ifdef CUSTOM_COST
708 const auto distance = getcij(initial_node_id, node_id);
709 double new_cost = cost_calculator.calculate_cost(initial_cost, distance, node_id, new_resources);
710#else
711 // Cache the distance value to avoid repeated calls
712 const auto distance = getcij(initial_node_id, node_id);
713 double new_cost = initial_cost + distance;
714#endif
715
716 const auto &VRPNode = nodes[node_id];
717
718 if constexpr (F != Full::PSTEP) {
719 new_cost -= VRPNode.cost;
720 } else {
721 if (L_prime->path_len > 1 && initial_node_id != options.depot) {
722 const auto three_two_dual = pstep_duals.getThreeTwoDualValue(initial_node_id);
723 new_cost += three_two_dual + pstep_duals.getThreeThreeDualValue(initial_node_id);
724 }
725 new_cost += -pstep_duals.getThreeTwoDualValue(node_id) + pstep_duals.getArcDualValue(initial_node_id, node_id);
726 }
727
728 // Apply branching dual adjustments if present - use local variables to
729 // avoid repeated pointer dereferencing
730 if (!branching_duals->empty()) {
731 if (D == Direction::Forward) {
732 new_cost += branching_duals->getDual(initial_node_id, node_id);
733 } else {
734 new_cost += branching_duals->getDual(node_id, initial_node_id);
735 }
736 new_cost += branching_duals->getDual(node_id);
737 }
738
739 RCC_MODE_BLOCK(if constexpr (S == Stage::Four) {
740 if (D == Direction::Forward) {
741 new_cost -= arc_duals.getDual(initial_node_id, node_id);
742 } else {
743 new_cost -= arc_duals.getDual(node_id, initial_node_id);
744 }
745 })
746
747 // Create and initialize a new label
748 auto &label_pool = assign_buckets<D>(label_pool_fw, label_pool_bw);
749 auto new_label = label_pool->acquire();
750 new_label->initialize(to_bucket, new_cost, new_resources, node_id);
751 new_label->vertex = to_bucket;
752 new_label->real_cost = L_prime->real_cost + distance; // Use cached distance
753 new_label->path_len = L_prime->path_len + 1;
754
755 // Update visited bitmap: intersect parent's visited bits with neighborhood
756 // mask
757 if constexpr (F != Full::PSTEP) {
758 for (size_t i = 0; i < new_label->visited_bitmap.size(); ++i) {
759 const uint64_t current_visited = L_prime->visited_bitmap[i];
760 if (current_visited) { new_label->visited_bitmap[i] = current_visited & neighborhoods_bitmap[node_id][i]; }
761 }
762 } else {
763 // Use memcpy for direct bitmap copy (likely faster than assignment for
764 // large bitmaps)
765 std::memcpy(new_label->visited_bitmap.data(), L_prime->visited_bitmap.data(),
766 new_label->visited_bitmap.size() * sizeof(uint64_t));
767 }
768
769 set_node_visited(new_label->visited_bitmap, node_id);
770
771 // Reuse pooled vector capacity when extending the route.
772 auto &extended_route = new_label->nodes_covered;
773 extended_route.clear();
774 extended_route.reserve(L_prime->nodes_covered.size() + 1);
775 extended_route.insert(extended_route.end(), L_prime->nodes_covered.begin(), L_prime->nodes_covered.end());
776 extended_route.push_back(node_id);
777
778#if defined(SRC)
779 if constexpr (S == Stage::Four || S == Stage::Enumerate) {
780 auto &cutter = cut_storage;
781 const auto n_cuts = cutter->activeSize();
782 if (__builtin_expect(n_cuts == 0, 0)) { // Branch prediction: unlikely
783 if constexpr (F == Full::Reverse) {
784 return -1;
785 } else {
786 return new_label;
787 }
788 }
789
790 new_label->SRCmap = L_prime->SRCmap;
791 const auto active_cuts = cutter->getActiveCuts();
792 double total_cost_update = 0.0;
793
794 // Prefetch critical data structures
795 __builtin_prefetch(active_cuts.data(), 0, 3);
796 __builtin_prefetch(new_label->SRCmap.data(), 1, 3); // 1 = read-write
797
798#if !defined(SRC_MEMORY_MODE_ARC)
799 auto &masks = cutter->getSegmentMasks();
800 __builtin_prefetch(&masks, 0, 3);
801 for (const auto &update : cutter->getSRCNodeUpdates(node_id)) {
802 auto &src_map_value = new_label->SRCmap[update.active_idx];
803 src_map_value += update.add;
804 const bool overflow = src_map_value >= update.den;
805 src_map_value -= overflow ? update.den : 0;
806 total_cost_update -= overflow ? update.dual : 0;
807 }
808
809 for (const auto active_idx : cutter->getSRCNodeClears(node_id)) { new_label->SRCmap[active_idx] = 0; }
810#else
811 for (const auto &active_cut : active_cuts) {
812 const auto &cut = *active_cut.cut_ptr;
813 auto &src_map_value = new_label->SRCmap[active_cut.index];
814 if (!cut.isSRCMemoryArc(L_prime->node_id, node_id)) { src_map_value = 0; }
815 if (cut.isSRCset(node_id)) {
816 src_map_value += cut.srcMultiplier(node_id);
817 if (src_map_value >= cut.p.den) {
818 src_map_value -= cut.p.den;
819 total_cost_update -= active_cut.dual_value;
820 }
821 }
822 }
823#endif
824
825 new_label->cost += total_cost_update;
826 }
827#endif
828 if constexpr (F != Full::Reverse) {
829 return new_label;
830 } else {
831 return -1;
832 }
833}

165#ifdef SCHRODINGER
166 if (merged_labels.size() > N_ADD) {
167 std::vector<Path> paths;
168 const int labels_size = merged_labels.size();

170 paths.reserve(end_idx - N_ADD);

179#endif

181#ifdef RIH

188 top_labels.push_back(merged_labels[i]);
189 }
190 ils->submit_task(top_labels, nodes);
191 }
192#endif
193
194 inner_obj = merged_labels[0]->cost;
195 return make_bucket_pricing_result(merged_labels);

◆ forbidCycle()

void BucketGraph::forbidCycle ( const std::vector< int > & cycle,
bool aggressive )

Forbids a cycle in the bucket graph.

This function takes a vector representing a cycle in the graph and forbids the edges corresponding to the cycle. If the 'aggressive' flag is set to true, it also forbids additional edges between the vertices of the cycle.

Definition at line 447 of file BucketGraph.cpp.

447 {
448 // If the cycle is too short, there is nothing to forbid.
449 if (cycle.size() < 2) return;
450
451 // Process each adjacent pair in the cycle.
452 for (size_t i = 0; i < cycle.size() - 1; ++i) {
453 int v1 = cycle[i];
454 int v2 = cycle[i + 1];
455
456 // Forbid v2 in the neighborhood of v1.
457 size_t segment = static_cast<size_t>(v2) >> 6;
458 size_t bit_position = static_cast<size_t>(v2) & 63;
459 neighborhoods_bitmap[v1][segment] |= bit_mask_lookup[bit_position];
460
461 if (aggressive) {
462 // Additionally, forbid v1 in the neighborhood of v2.
463 segment = static_cast<size_t>(v1) >> 6;
464 bit_position = static_cast<size_t>(v1) & 63;
465 neighborhoods_bitmap[v2][segment] |= bit_mask_lookup[bit_position];
466 }
467 }
468}

12 {

14 fw_bucket_graph.clear();
15 fw_arcs.clear();

19 }
20
21 auto &buckets = assign_buckets<D>(fw_buckets, bw_buckets);
22 auto &num_buckets = assign_buckets<D>(num_buckets_fw, num_buckets_bw);
23 auto &num_buckets_idx = assign_buckets<D>(num_buckets_index_fw, num_buckets_index_bw);
24
25 const int num_resources = options.resources.size();
26 std::vector<std::vector<double>> node_base_intervals(nodes.size());
27 std::vector<std::vector<int>> node_split_counts(nodes.size());
28
29 for (size_t node_id = 0; node_id < nodes.size(); ++node_id) {
30 const auto &node = nodes[node_id];
31 node_base_intervals[node_id].resize(num_resources);
32 node_split_counts[node_id].resize(num_resources);
33 for (int r = 0; r < num_resources; ++r) {
34 double full_range = R_max[r] - R_min[r];
35 double node_range = node.ub[r] - node.lb[r];
36 int splits = 1;
37 if (std::fabs(full_range) > std::numeric_limits<double>::epsilon()) {
38 double splits_d = (node_range * intervals[r].interval) / full_range;
39 splits = std::max(1, static_cast<int>(std::round(splits_d)));
40 }
41 node_split_counts[node_id][r] = splits;
42 node_base_intervals[node_id][r] = (node.ub[r] - node.lb[r]) / static_cast<double>(splits);
43 }
44 }
45
46 for (auto &bucket : buckets) {
47 bucket.clear();
48 bucket.clear_arcs(D == Direction::Forward);
49 }
50
51 auto try_add_arc = [&](int from_bucket, const VRPNode &next_node, const std::vector<double> &res_inc,
52 double cost_inc) -> bool {
53 bool valid = true;
54 std::vector<double> head_resource(res_inc.size(), 0.0);
55 if constexpr (D == Direction::Forward) {
56 for (int r = 0; r < res_inc.size() && valid; ++r) {
57 if (numericutils::gt(buckets[from_bucket].lb[r] + res_inc[r], next_node.ub[r])) {
58 valid = false;
59 } else {
60 head_resource[r] = std::max(buckets[from_bucket].lb[r] + res_inc[r], next_node.lb[r]);
61 }
62 }
63 } else {
64 for (int r = 0; r < res_inc.size() && valid; ++r) {
65 if (numericutils::lt(buckets[from_bucket].ub[r] - res_inc[r], next_node.lb[r])) {
66 valid = false;
67 } else {
68 head_resource[r] = std::min(buckets[from_bucket].ub[r] - res_inc[r], next_node.ub[r]);
69 }
70 }
71 }
72 if (valid) {
73 const int to_bucket = get_bucket_number<D>(next_node.id, head_resource);
74 buckets[from_bucket].template add_bucket_arc<D>(from_bucket, to_bucket, res_inc, cost_inc, false);
75 }
76 return valid;
77 };
78
79 auto process_node = [&](int node_id) {
80 const auto &node = nodes[node_id];
81 std::vector<double> res_inc(num_resources);
82 const auto arcs = node.get_arcs<D>();
83
84 for (int i = 0; i < num_buckets[node.id]; ++i) {
85 int from_bucket = i + num_buckets_idx[node.id];
86 for (const auto &arc : arcs) {
87 const auto &next_node = nodes[arc.to];
88 if (node.id == next_node.id) continue;
89
90 const double travel_cost = getcij(node.id, next_node.id);
91 double cost_inc = travel_cost - next_node.cost;
92 for (int r = 0; r < num_resources; r++) {
93 res_inc[r] = node.consumption[r];
94 if (options.resources[r] == "time") { res_inc[r] += travel_cost; }
95 }
96
97 try_add_arc(from_bucket, next_node, res_inc, cost_inc);
98 }
99 }
100 };
101
102 std::vector<int> tasks(nodes.size());
103 std::iota(tasks.begin(), tasks.end(), 0);
104
105 constexpr int chunk_size = 10;
106 const unsigned int thread_count = std::max(1u, std::thread::hardware_concurrency() / 2);
107 exec::static_thread_pool pool(thread_count);
108 auto sched = pool.get_scheduler();
109
110 auto bulk_sender = stdexec::bulk(stdexec::just(), (tasks.size() + chunk_size - 1) / chunk_size,
111 [&, chunk_size](std::size_t chunk_idx) {
112 const size_t start_idx = chunk_idx * chunk_size;
113 const size_t end_idx = std::min(start_idx + chunk_size, tasks.size());
114 for (size_t idx = start_idx; idx < end_idx; ++idx) {
115 process_node(tasks[idx]);
116 }
117 });
118
119 auto work = stdexec::starts_on(sched, bulk_sender);
120 stdexec::sync_wait(std::move(work));
121
122 auto &bucket_graph = assign_buckets<D>(fw_bucket_graph, bw_bucket_graph);
123 auto &global_arcs = assign_buckets<D>(fw_arcs, bw_arcs);
124 bucket_graph.assign(buckets.size(), {});
125 global_arcs.clear();
126
127 size_t arc_count = 0;
128 for (int b = 0; b < static_cast<int>(buckets.size()); ++b) {
129 const auto &bucket_arcs = buckets[b].template get_bucket_arcs<D>();
130 arc_count += bucket_arcs.size();
131 }
132 global_arcs.reserve(arc_count);
133
134 for (int b = 0; b < static_cast<int>(buckets.size()); ++b) {
135 auto &neighbors = bucket_graph[b];
136 const auto &bucket_arcs = buckets[b].template get_bucket_arcs<D>();
137 neighbors.reserve(bucket_arcs.size());
138 for (const auto &arc : bucket_arcs) {
139 neighbors.push_back(arc.to_bucket);
140 global_arcs.push_back(arc);
141 }
142 }
143}
Here is the call graph for this function:

◆ generate_arcs() [2/2]

void BucketGraph::generate_arcs ( )

Generates arcs in both forward and backward directions in parallel.

This function uses OpenMP to parallelize the generation of arcs in both forward and backward directions. It performs the following steps for each direction:

  • Calls the generate_arcs function template with the appropriate direction.
  • Clears and resizes the Phi vector for the respective direction.
  • Computes the Phi values for each bucket and stores them in the Phi vector.
  • Calls the SCC_handler function template with the appropriate direction.

The forward direction operations are performed in one OpenMP section, and the backward direction operations are performed in another OpenMP section.

Definition at line 866 of file BucketGraph.cpp.

866 {
867 PARALLEL_SECTIONS(
868 work, bi_sched,
869 SECTION {
870 // Task for Forward Direction
871 generate_arcs<Direction::Forward>();
872 Phi_fw.clear();
873 Phi_fw.resize(fw_buckets_size);
874 for (int i = 0; i < fw_buckets_size; ++i) { Phi_fw[i] = computePhi(i, true); }
875 SCC_handler<Direction::Forward>();
876 },
877 SECTION {
878 // Task for Backward Direction
879 generate_arcs<Direction::Backward>();
880 Phi_bw.clear();
881 Phi_bw.resize(bw_buckets_size);
882 for (int i = 0; i < bw_buckets_size; ++i) { Phi_bw[i] = computePhi(i, false); }
883 SCC_handler<Direction::Backward>();
884 });
885}

AdjList BucketGraph::get_adjacency_list ( )

1484 {
1485 AdjList adjacency_list;
1486
1487 // Iterate through all nodes in the graph
1488 for (const auto &node : nodes) {
1489 std::vector<std::tuple<int, double, int>> arcs;
1490
1491 // Retrieve all arcs associated with the current node
1492 for (const auto &arc : node.get_arcs<Direction::Forward>()) {
1493 int to_node = arc.to;
1494 double cost = getcij(node.id, to_node) - nodes[to_node].cost;
1495 int capacity = 1;
1496
1497 // Only add arcs with non-zero capacity
1498 if (capacity > 0) { arcs.emplace_back(to_node, cost, capacity); }
1499 }
1500
1501 // Add the node's arcs to the adjacency list if it has
1502 // any outgoing edges
1503 if (!arcs.empty()) { adjacency_list[node.id] = arcs; }
1504 }
1505
1506 return adjacency_list;

81 int bucket_number = num_buckets_index[node];
82 int multiplier = 1;
83
84 for (size_t r = 0; r < num_resources; ++r) {
85 // Node-specific resource bounds.
86 double node_lb = nodes[node].lb[r];
87 double node_ub = nodes[node].ub[r];
88
89 // Global bounds (if needed for computing splits).
90 double global_lb = R_min[r];
91 double global_ub = R_max[r];
92 double full_range = global_ub - global_lb;
93 double node_range = node_ub - node_lb;
94
95 // Determine the number of splits (i.e. intervals) for resource r.
96 int splits = std::max(1, static_cast<int>(std::round((node_range / full_range) * intervals[r].interval)));
97 double interval_width =
98 (splits == 0 || std::fabs(node_range) < std::numeric_limits<double>::epsilon()) ? 1.0 : node_range / splits;
99
100 int pos = 0;
101 if constexpr (D == Direction::Forward) {
102 if (std::fabs(interval_width) < std::numeric_limits<double>::epsilon()) {
103 pos = 0;
104 } else {
105 pos = std::clamp(static_cast<int>(std::floor((resource_values_vec[r] - node_lb) / interval_width)), 0,
106 splits - 1);
107 }
108 } else {
109 if (std::fabs(interval_width) < std::numeric_limits<double>::epsilon()) {
110 pos = 0;
111 } else {
112 pos = std::clamp(static_cast<int>(std::floor((node_ub - resource_values_vec[r]) / interval_width)), 0,
113 splits - 1);
114 }
115 }
116
117 bucket_number += pos * multiplier;
118 multiplier *= splits;
119 }
120
121 // check if buckets[bucket_number] contains the resource_vec
122 if (unlikely(!buckets[bucket_number].contains(resource_values_vec))) {
123 // print resource_values_vec
124 for (auto val : resource_values_vec) { fmt::print("{}\n", val); }
125 // print bucket bounds
126 fmt::print("{}\n", buckets[bucket_number].lb[0]);
127 fmt::print("{}\n", buckets[bucket_number].ub[0]);
128
129 std::throw_with_nested(std::runtime_error("Resource values not contained in bucket"));
130 return -1;
131 }
132
133 return bucket_number;
134}

◆ get_opposite_bucket_number()

template<Direction D>
int BucketGraph::get_opposite_bucket_number ( int current_bucket_index,
std::vector< double > & inc )

Get the opposite bucket number for a given bucket index.

This function retrieves the opposite bucket number based on the current bucket index and the specified direction. It determines the node and bounds of the current bucket, then calculates the opposite bucket index using the appropriate direction.

Definition at line 409 of file BucketUtils.h.

409 {
410 // Retrieve the current bucket based on the current direction.
411 const auto &current_bucket =
412 (D == Direction::Forward) ? fw_buckets[current_bucket_index] : bw_buckets[current_bucket_index];
413 const int node = current_bucket.node_id;
414 const auto &theNode = nodes[node];
415
416 // Pre-compute the reference point for the given node using the main
417 // resources.
418 const size_t num_resources = options.main_resources.size();
419 // Optionally, add an assertion or check that inc has the expected size.
420 assert(inc.size() == num_resources && theNode.lb.size() == num_resources && theNode.ub.size() == num_resources);
421
422 std::vector<double> reference_point(num_resources);
423
424 if constexpr (D == Direction::Forward) {
425 // For Forward direction:
426 // Use the maximum of inc and the node's lower bounds.
427 // This ensures we do not fall below the allowed lower bound.
428 for (size_t i = 0; i < num_resources; ++i) {
429 reference_point[i] = std::max(inc[i], theNode.lb[i]);
430 // Optionally, adjust with epsilon if needed:
431 // reference_point[i] += numericutils::eps;
432 }
433 // Get the opposite bucket using the Backward tree.
434 return get_bucket_number<Direction::Backward>(node, reference_point);
435 } else {
436 // For Backward direction:
437 // Use the minimum of inc and the node's upper bounds.
438 // This ensures we do not exceed the allowed upper bound.
439 for (size_t i = 0; i < num_resources; ++i) {
440 reference_point[i] = std::min(inc[i], theNode.ub[i]);
441 // Optionally, adjust with epsilon if needed:
442 // reference_point[i] -= numericutils::eps;
443 }
444 // Get the opposite bucket using the Forward tree.
445 return get_bucket_number<Direction::Forward>(node, reference_point);
446 }
447}

◆ heuristic_fixing()

template<Stage S>
void BucketGraph::heuristic_fixing ( )

Applies heuristic fixing to the current solution.

This function modifies the current solution based on the heuristic fixing strategy using the provided vector of values.

526
