538 // Run the appropriate labeling algorithms based on stage
539 if (S == Stage::Three) {
540 // Run the labeling algorithms for Stage::Two with Full::Partial mode.
541 run_labeling_algorithms<Stage::Two, Full::Partial>(forward_cbar, backward_cbar);
542 } else if (S == Stage::Four) {
543 fmt::print("\033[34m_STARTING ARC FIXING PROCEDURE\033[0m\n");
544 fmt::print("gap: {}\n", gap);
545 // Run the labeling algorithms for Stage::Four with Full::Full mode.
546 run_labeling_algorithms<Stage::Four, Full::Full>(forward_cbar, backward_cbar);
547 }
548
549 // Prepare containers for grouping labels by node.
550 const size_t num_nodes = nodes.size();
551
552 // Preallocate vectors with nullptr to avoid resizing
553 std::vector<const Label *> min_fw_labels(num_nodes, nullptr);
554 std::vector<const Label *> min_bw_labels(num_nodes, nullptr);
555
556 // Find minimum cost label for each node directly from buckets
557 // without creating intermediate label maps
558 for (auto &bucket : fw_buckets) {
559 for (const Label *label : bucket.get_labels()) {
560 if (label == nullptr || label->is_dominated) continue;
561 const size_t node_id = label->node_id;
562 if (!min_fw_labels[node_id] || label->cost < min_fw_labels[node_id]->cost) {
563 min_fw_labels[node_id] = label;
564 }
565 }
566 }
567
568 for (auto &bucket : bw_buckets) {
569 for (const Label *label : bucket.get_labels()) {
570 if (label == nullptr || label->is_dominated) continue;
571 const size_t node_id = label->node_id;
572 if (!min_bw_labels[node_id] || label->cost < min_bw_labels[node_id]->cost) {
573 min_bw_labels[node_id] = label;
574 }
575 }
576 }
577
578 // Pre-compute the index of the "time" resource.
579 const size_t time_index =
580 std::distance(options.resources.begin(), std::find(options.resources.begin(), options.resources.end(), "time"));
581
582 // Precompute lookup tables for common values
583 std::vector<double> fw_time_resources(num_nodes, 0.0);
584 std::vector<double> bw_time_resources(num_nodes, 0.0);
585 std::vector<double> fw_costs(num_nodes, 0.0);
586 std::vector<double> bw_costs(num_nodes, 0.0);
587
588 for (size_t i = 0; i < num_nodes; ++i) {
589 if (min_fw_labels[i]) {
590 fw_time_resources[i] = min_fw_labels[i]->resources[time_index];
591 fw_costs[i] = min_fw_labels[i]->cost;
592 }
593 if (min_bw_labels[i]) {
594 bw_time_resources[i] = min_bw_labels[i]->resources[time_index];
595 bw_costs[i] = min_bw_labels[i]->cost;
596 }
597 }
598
599 // Cache node data to avoid repeated lookups
600 std::vector<double> node_durations(num_nodes);
601 std::vector<std::vector<double>> node_consumptions(num_nodes, std::vector<double>(options.resources.size()));
602
603 for (size_t i = 0; i < num_nodes; ++i) {
604 const VRPNode &node = nodes[i];
605 node_durations[i] = node.duration;
606 for (size_t r = 0; r < options.resources.size(); ++r) { node_consumptions[i][r] = node.consumption[r]; }
607 }
608
609 size_t num_fixes = 0;
610 const double gap_threshold = gap; // Cache this value
611
612 // Build a list of candidate node pairs to check
613 struct NodePair {
614 size_t i;
615 size_t j;
616 };
617
618 std::vector<NodePair> candidate_pairs;
619 candidate_pairs.reserve(num_nodes * num_nodes / 4); // Estimate: 25% of pairs will be candidates
620
621 for (size_t i = 0; i < num_nodes; ++i) {
622 if (!min_fw_labels[i]) continue;
623
624 for (size_t j = 0; j < num_nodes; ++j) {
625 if (i == j || !min_bw_labels[j]) continue;
626 candidate_pairs.push_back({i, j});
627 }
628 }
629
630 // Process candidate pairs in batches to improve cache locality
631 const size_t BATCH_SIZE = 256;
632 std::vector<std::pair<size_t, size_t>> arcs_to_fix;
633 arcs_to_fix.reserve(BATCH_SIZE);
634
635 for (size_t start = 0; start < candidate_pairs.size(); start += BATCH_SIZE) {
636 const size_t end = std::min(start + BATCH_SIZE, candidate_pairs.size());
637 arcs_to_fix.clear();
638
639 for (size_t idx = start; idx < end; ++idx) {
640 const auto &pair = candidate_pairs[idx];
641 const size_t i = pair.i;
642 const size_t j = pair.j;
643
644 const double cost = getcij(min_fw_labels[i]->node_id, min_bw_labels[j]->node_id);
645
646 // Early rejection using precomputed time resources (often most
647 // restrictive)
648 if (options.resources[time_index] == "time") {
649 if (fw_time_resources[i] + cost + node_durations[min_fw_labels[i]->node_id] > bw_time_resources[j]) {
650 continue;
651 }
652 }
653
654 // Check all other resources
655 bool violated = false;
656 const size_t fw_node_id = min_fw_labels[i]->node_id;
657
658 for (size_t r = 0; r < options.resources.size(); ++r) {
659 if (r != time_index) {
660 if (min_fw_labels[i]->resources[r] + node_consumptions[fw_node_id][r] >
661 min_bw_labels[j]->resources[r]) {
662 violated = true;
663 break;
664 }
665 }
666 }
667
668 // If all resource constraints are satisfied and the cost exceeds
669 // the gap, fix the arc
670 if (!violated && (fw_costs[i] + cost + bw_costs[j] > gap_threshold)) {
671 arcs_to_fix.emplace_back(i, j);
672 ++num_fixes;
673 }
674 }
675
676 // Fix arcs in batch
677 for (const auto &[i, j] : arcs_to_fix) { fix_arc(i, j); }
678 }
679
680 if (S == Stage::Four) {
681 fmt::print("Applied {} arc fixes\n", num_fixes);
682 fmt::print("\033[34m_ARC FIXING PROCEDURE FINISHED\033[0m\n");
683 }
684}

◆ initInfo()

void BucketGraph::initInfo ( )
static

Prints the configuration information of the BucketGraph.

This function outputs the configuration details including resource size, number of clients, and maximum SRC cuts. It also conditionally prints whether RIH, RCC, and SRC are enabled or disabled based on the preprocessor directives.

The output format is as follows:

+-------------------------------—+ | CONFIGURATION INFO | +-------------------------------—+ Resources: <R_SIZE> Number of Clients: <N_SIZE> Maximum SRC cuts: <MAX_SRC_CUTS> RIH: <enabled/disabled> RCC: <enabled/disabled> SRC: <enabled/disabled> +-------------------------------—+

Definition at line 963 of file BucketGraph.cpp.

963 {
964 // Print header
965 fmt::print("\n+----------------------------------+\n");
966 fmt::print("| CONFIGURATION INFO |\n");
967 fmt::print("+----------------------------------+\n");
968
969 // Print Resource size
970 fmt::print("Resources: {}\n", R_SIZE);
971 fmt::print("Number of Clients: {}\n", N_SIZE - 2);
972
973 // Conditional configuration (RIH enabled/disabled)
974#ifdef RIH
975 fmt::print("RIH: enabled\n");
976#else
977 fmt::print("RIH: disabled\n");
978#endif
979#ifdef RCC
980 fmt::print("RCC: enabled\n");
981#else
982 fmt::print("RCC: disabled\n");
983#endif
984#ifdef SRC
985 fmt::print("SRC: enabled\n");
986#else
987 fmt::print("SRC: disabled\n");
988#endif
989 fmt::print("+----------------------------------+\n");
990
991 fmt::print("\n");
992}

888 // A label cannot dominate if its cost is higher.

892 if (numericutils::gt(label->cost, new_label->cost)) { return false; }
893 }
894#else

896#endif
897

901 // For Stage Four or Enumerate, apply additional SRC-based cost
902 // adjustments.
903 if constexpr (S == Stage::Four || S == Stage::Enumerate) {
904 const double local_cost_diff = label->cost - new_label->cost;
905 const auto *__restrict lbl_srcs = label->SRCmap.data();
906 const auto *__restrict new_lbl_srcs = new_label->SRCmap.data();
907

914 const size_t n = active_cuts.size();
915 for (size_t i = 0; i < n; ++i) {
916 const auto &cut = active_cuts[i];
917 // Branch prediction hint: likely false.
918 if (__builtin_expect(lbl_srcs[cut.index] > new_lbl_srcs[cut.index], 0)) { dual_sum += cut.dual_value; }
919 }
920 return !numericutils::gt(local_cost_diff, dual_sum);
921 }
922#endif
923 return true;
924}

◆ is_node_unreachable()

bool BucketGraph::is_node_unreachable ( const std::array< uint64_t, num_words > & bitmap,
int node_id )
inlinestatic

Checks if a node is unreachable based on a bitmap.

This function determines if a node, identified by its node_id, is unreachable by checking a specific bit in a bitmap. The bitmap is represented as an array of 64-bit unsigned integers.

754 {
755 int word_index = node_id >> 6; // Determine which 64-bit segment

757 int bit_position = node_id & 63; // Determine the bit position within that segment

759 }

◆ is_node_visited()

bool BucketGraph::is_node_visited ( const std::array< uint64_t, num_words > & bitmap,
int node_id )
inlinestatic

Checks if a node has been visited based on a bitmap.

This function determines if a specific node, identified by node_id, has been visited by checking the corresponding bit in a bitmap array. The bitmap is an array of 64-bit unsigned integers, where each bit represents the visited status of a node.

◆ labeling_algorithm()

template<Direction D, Stage S, Full F>
std::vector< double > BucketGraph::labeling_algorithm ( )

Performs the labeling algorithm on the BucketGraph.

Definition at line 79 of file BucketSolve.h.

79 {
80 // Cache bucket and auxiliary structure references to avoid repeated
81 // lookups.
82 auto &buckets = assign_buckets<D>(fw_buckets, bw_buckets);
83 const auto &topological_order = assign_buckets<D>(fw_topological_order, bw_topological_order);
84 const auto &sccs = assign_buckets<D>(fw_sccs, bw_sccs);
85 const auto &Phi = assign_buckets<D>(Phi_fw, Phi_bw);
86 auto &c_bar = assign_buckets<D>(fw_c_bar, bw_c_bar);
87 auto &n_labels = assign_buckets<D>(n_fw_labels, n_bw_labels);
88 const auto &sorted_sccs = assign_buckets<D>(fw_sccs_sorted, bw_sccs_sorted);
89 const auto &n_buckets = assign_buckets<D>(fw_buckets_size, bw_buckets_size);
90 auto &stat_n_labels = assign_buckets<D>(stat_n_labels_fw, stat_n_labels_bw);
91 auto &stat_n_dom = assign_buckets<D>(stat_n_dom_fw, stat_n_dom_bw);
92 auto &arc_scores = assign_buckets<D>(fw_arc_scores, bw_arc_scores);
93 auto &best_label = assign_buckets<D>(fw_best_label, bw_best_label);
94
95 const bool profile_labeling = options.profile_labeling;
96
97 // Reset the total label count.
98 n_labels = 0;
99
100 // Pre-calculate bitmap segments for dominance-checking.
101 const size_t n_segments = (n_buckets + 63) >> 6;
102 std::vector<uint64_t> Bvisited(n_segments, 0);
103 std::vector<uint32_t> touched_segments;
104 touched_segments.reserve(std::min<size_t>(n_segments, 64));
105 std::vector<int> bucket_pos_in_scc(n_buckets, -1);
106
107 // Process only newly queued labels while preserving the bucket order
108 // inside each SCC.
109 std::vector<std::vector<Label *>> pending_labels_by_bucket;
110 std::vector<size_t> pending_label_cursor;
111 std::vector<uint8_t> bucket_is_active;
112 std::vector<int> active_bucket_heap;
113
114 // Precompute main resource index and q_star value for partial solutions.
115 constexpr bool is_partial = (F == Full::Partial);
116 int main_res_index = 0;
117 double q_star_value = 0.0;
118 if constexpr (is_partial) {
119 main_res_index = options.main_resources[0];
120 q_star_value = std::floor(q_star[main_res_index]);
121 }
122
123 // Reuse a thread-local vector for collecting labels to dominate.
124 static thread_local std::vector<Label *> tl_labels_to_dominate;
125 tl_labels_to_dominate.clear();
126 tl_labels_to_dominate.reserve(64); // Pre-reserve space to avoid reallocations
127
128 // Process each strongly connected component (SCC) in topological order.
129 for (const auto &scc_index : topological_order) {
130 const auto &scc_buckets = sorted_sccs[scc_index];
131 if (scc_buckets.empty()) continue;
132
133 pending_labels_by_bucket.clear();
134 pending_labels_by_bucket.resize(scc_buckets.size());
135 pending_label_cursor.assign(scc_buckets.size(), 0);
136 bucket_is_active.assign(scc_buckets.size(), 0);
137 active_bucket_heap.clear();
138
139 auto activate_bucket = [&](int bucket_pos) {
140 if (bucket_pos < 0 || bucket_is_active[bucket_pos]) return;
141 bucket_is_active[bucket_pos] = 1;
142 active_bucket_heap.push_back(bucket_pos);
143 std::push_heap(active_bucket_heap.begin(), active_bucket_heap.end(), std::greater<int>());
144 };
145
146 for (size_t bucket_idx = 0; bucket_idx < scc_buckets.size(); ++bucket_idx) {
147 const int bucket = scc_buckets[bucket_idx];
148 bucket_pos_in_scc[bucket] = static_cast<int>(bucket_idx);
149
150 const auto &bucket_labels = buckets[bucket].get_sorted_labels();
151 const auto &extra_labels = buckets[bucket].get_extra_labels();
152 auto &pending_labels = pending_labels_by_bucket[bucket_idx];
153 pending_labels.reserve(bucket_labels.size() + extra_labels.size());
154
155 for (Label *label : bucket_labels) {
156 if (!label->is_extended && !label->is_dominated) pending_labels.push_back(label);
157 }
158 for (Label *label : extra_labels) {
159 if (!label->is_extended && !label->is_dominated) pending_labels.push_back(label);
160 }
161
162 if (!pending_labels.empty()) activate_bucket(static_cast<int>(bucket_idx));
163 }
164
165 while (!active_bucket_heap.empty()) {
166 std::pop_heap(active_bucket_heap.begin(), active_bucket_heap.end(), std::greater<int>());
167 const int bucket_idx = active_bucket_heap.back();
168 active_bucket_heap.pop_back();
169
170 const int bucket = scc_buckets[bucket_idx];
171 auto &pending_labels = pending_labels_by_bucket[bucket_idx];
172 size_t &pending_cursor = pending_label_cursor[bucket_idx];
173
174 while (pending_cursor < pending_labels.size()) {
175 Label *label = pending_labels[pending_cursor++];
176
177 // Skip labels already processed.
178 if (__builtin_expect(label->is_extended || label->is_dominated, 0)) continue;
179
180 // For partial solutions, check resource feasibility.
181 if constexpr (F != Full::PSTEP && F != Full::TSP) {
182 if constexpr (is_partial) {
183 const auto main_resource = label->resources[main_res_index];
184 if ((D == Direction::Forward && main_resource > q_star_value) ||
185 (D == Direction::Backward && main_resource <= q_star_value)) {
186 label->set_extended(true);
187 continue;
188 }
189 }
190 }
191
192 for (uint32_t segment_idx : touched_segments) { Bvisited[segment_idx] = 0; }
193 touched_segments.clear();
194
195 // Check if the label is dominated by labels in smaller
196 // buckets.
197 if constexpr (F != Full::TSP) {
198 if (profile_labeling) profile_record_dominance_check(D, S);
199 if (DominatedInCompWiseSmallerBuckets<D, S>(label, bucket, c_bar, Bvisited, touched_segments,
200 stat_n_dom))
201 continue;
202 }
203
204 // Retrieve outgoing arcs for the current label.
205 const auto &node_arcs = buckets[bucket].template get_bucket_arcs<D>();
206 if (__builtin_expect(node_arcs.empty(), 0)) {
207 label->set_extended(true);
208 continue;
209 }
210
211 // Prefetch first few arcs
212 if (!node_arcs.empty()) {
213 for (size_t i = 0; i < std::min(size_t(4), node_arcs.size()); ++i) {
214 __builtin_prefetch(&node_arcs[i], 0, 3);
215 }
216 }
217
218 for (size_t arc_idx = 0; arc_idx < node_arcs.size(); ++arc_idx) {
219 const auto &arc = node_arcs[arc_idx];
220
221 // Prefetch next arc if available
222 if (arc_idx + 4 < node_arcs.size()) { __builtin_prefetch(&node_arcs[arc_idx + 4], 0, 3); }
223
224 auto new_label = Extend<D, S, ArcType::Bucket, Mutability::Mut, F>(label, arc);
225
226 // Process each new label produced by the extension.
227 if (new_label != nullptr) {
228 if constexpr (S == Stage::Enumerate) {
229 if (n_labels >= enumeration_policy.max_labels_per_direction) {
230 enumeration_failed.store(true, std::memory_order_relaxed);
231 label->set_extended(true);
232 continue;
233 }
234 }
235 ++stat_n_labels;
236 const int to_bucket = new_label->vertex;
237 auto &mother_bucket = buckets[to_bucket];
238 uint64_t bucket_scan_count = 0;
239
240 if (profile_labeling) { profile_record_new_label(D, S); }
241
242 // Prefetch the mother bucket and its labels
243 __builtin_prefetch(&mother_bucket, 0, 3);
244
245 mother_bucket.flush_extra_labels_if_large();
246 const auto &to_bucket_labels = mother_bucket.get_sorted_labels();
247 const auto &to_bucket_extra = mother_bucket.get_extra_labels();
248
249 // Prefetch first few destination bucket labels
250 if (!to_bucket_labels.empty()) {
251 for (size_t i = 0; i < std::min(size_t(4), to_bucket_labels.size()); ++i) {
252 __builtin_prefetch(to_bucket_labels[i], 0, 3);
253 }
254 }
255
256 bool dominated = false;
257 if constexpr (S == Stage::One) {
258 // Stage One: mark higher-cost labels as
259 // dominated.
260 for (auto *existing_label : to_bucket_labels) {
261 ++bucket_scan_count;
262 if (existing_label->is_dominated) continue;
263 if (new_label->cost < existing_label->cost)
264 existing_label->set_dominated(true);
265 else {
266 dominated = true;
267 break;
268 }
269 }
270 if (!dominated) {
271 for (auto *existing_label : to_bucket_extra) {
272 ++bucket_scan_count;
273 if (existing_label->is_dominated) continue;
274 if (new_label->cost < existing_label->cost)
275 existing_label->set_dominated(true);
276 else {
277 dominated = true;
278 break;
279 }
280 }
281 }
282 if (profile_labeling) {
283 profile_record_dominance_check(D, S);
284 profile_record_inner_bin_scan(D, S, bucket_scan_count);
285 if (D == Direction::Forward) {
286 dominance_checks_per_bucket[to_bucket] += static_cast<int>(bucket_scan_count);
287 }
288 }
289 } else {
290 uint64_t inner_scan_count = 0;
291
292#if defined(SRC)
293 constexpr bool src_adjusted_dominance = (S == Stage::Four || S == Stage::Enumerate);
294#else
295 constexpr bool src_adjusted_dominance = false;
296#endif
297 // RC-bracketed single-pass dominance (RouteOpt
298 // dominance.hpp doDominance style). The committed
299 // tier is RC-sorted; for non-SRC stages, raw cost
300 // decides which dominance direction is possible:
301 // cur.cost < new.cost - eps: only cur can
302 // dominate new, so test resource/path dominance
303 // of new by cur; stop if true.
304 // cur.cost > new.cost + eps: only new can
305 // dominate cur, so test resource/path dominance
306 // of cur by new; mark cur dominated if true.
307 // near-equal costs: test both full predicates.
308 // SRC stages skip this bracketing because SRC dual
309 // compensation can overturn raw reduced-cost order.
310 const double cost_lo = new_label->cost - numericutils::eps;
311 const double cost_hi = new_label->cost + numericutils::eps;
312
313 auto label_cost_less = [](const Label *label, double value) { return label->cost < value; };
314
315 auto scan_new_dominated_by = [&](size_t begin, size_t end) {
316 for (size_t i = begin; i < end; ++i) {
317 if (i + 8 < end) { __builtin_prefetch(to_bucket_labels[i + 8], 0, 3); }
318 Label *cur = to_bucket_labels[i];
319 ++inner_scan_count;
320 if (__builtin_expect(cur->is_dominated, 0)) continue;
321 if (dominates_resource_path<D, S>(new_label, cur)) {
322 ++stat_n_dom;
323 dominated = true;
324 break;
325 }
326 }
327 };
328
329 auto scan_existing_dominated_by_new = [&](size_t begin, size_t end) {
330 for (size_t i = begin; i < end; ++i) {
331 if (i + 8 < end) { __builtin_prefetch(to_bucket_labels[i + 8], 0, 3); }
332 Label *cur = to_bucket_labels[i];
333 ++inner_scan_count;
334 if (__builtin_expect(cur->is_dominated, 0)) continue;
335 if (dominates_resource_path<D, S>(cur, new_label)) { cur->set_dominated(true); }
336 }
337 };
338
339 auto scan_bracket = [&](size_t begin, size_t end) {
340 for (size_t i = begin; i < end; ++i) {
341 if (i + 8 < end) { __builtin_prefetch(to_bucket_labels[i + 8], 0, 3); }
342 Label *cur = to_bucket_labels[i];
343 ++inner_scan_count;
344 if (__builtin_expect(cur->is_dominated, 0)) continue;
345 if (is_dominated<D, S>(new_label, cur)) {
346 ++stat_n_dom;
347 dominated = true;
348 break;
349 }
350 if (is_dominated<D, S>(cur, new_label)) { cur->set_dominated(true); }
351 }
352 };
353
354 // Forward path: walk sorted committed labels once.
355 const size_t to_bucket_size = to_bucket_labels.size();
356 if constexpr (src_adjusted_dominance) {
357 for (size_t i = 0; i < to_bucket_size; ++i) {
358 if (i + 8 < to_bucket_size) { __builtin_prefetch(to_bucket_labels[i + 8], 0, 3); }
359 Label *cur = to_bucket_labels[i];
360 ++inner_scan_count;
361 if (__builtin_expect(cur->is_dominated, 0)) continue;
362 if (is_dominated<D, S>(new_label, cur)) {
363 ++stat_n_dom;
364 dominated = true;
365 break;
366 }
367 if (is_dominated<D, S>(cur, new_label)) { cur->set_dominated(true); }
368 }
369 } else {
370 static constexpr size_t kLinearCostBracketScanLimit = 32;
371 if (to_bucket_size <= kLinearCostBracketScanLimit) {
372 for (size_t i = 0; i < to_bucket_size; ++i) {
373 if (i + 8 < to_bucket_size) {
374 __builtin_prefetch(to_bucket_labels[i + 8], 0, 3);
375 }
376 Label *cur = to_bucket_labels[i];
377 ++inner_scan_count;
378 if (__builtin_expect(cur->is_dominated, 0)) continue;
379 const double cur_cost = cur->cost;
380 if (cur_cost < cost_lo) {
381 if (dominates_resource_path<D, S>(new_label, cur)) {
382 ++stat_n_dom;
383 dominated = true;
384 break;
385 }
386 } else if (cur_cost > cost_hi) {
387 if (dominates_resource_path<D, S>(cur, new_label)) {
388 cur->set_dominated(true);
389 }
390 } else {
391 if (is_dominated<D, S>(new_label, cur)) {
392 ++stat_n_dom;
393 dominated = true;
394 break;
395 }
396 if (is_dominated<D, S>(cur, new_label)) { cur->set_dominated(true); }
397 }
398 }
399 } else {
400 const auto bracket_begin_it = std::lower_bound(
401 to_bucket_labels.begin(), to_bucket_labels.end(), cost_lo, label_cost_less);
402 const auto expensive_begin_it = std::upper_bound(
403 to_bucket_labels.begin(), to_bucket_labels.end(), cost_hi,
404 [](double value, const Label *label) { return value < label->cost; });
405 const size_t bracket_begin =
406 static_cast<size_t>(std::distance(to_bucket_labels.begin(), bracket_begin_it));
407 const size_t expensive_begin = static_cast<size_t>(
408 std::distance(to_bucket_labels.begin(), expensive_begin_it));
409
410 scan_new_dominated_by(0, bracket_begin);
411 if (!dominated) { scan_bracket(bracket_begin, expensive_begin); }
412 if (!dominated) { scan_existing_dominated_by_new(expensive_begin, to_bucket_size); }
413 }
414 }
415
416 // Extra (unsorted) tier: same trichotomy without
417 // sort assumptions. Only walked if not dominated
418 // by the sorted tier.
419 if (!dominated) {
420 for (Label *cur : to_bucket_extra) {
421 ++inner_scan_count;
422 if (__builtin_expect(cur->is_dominated, 0)) continue;
423 const double cur_cost = cur->cost;
424 if constexpr (src_adjusted_dominance) {
425 if (is_dominated<D, S>(new_label, cur)) {
426 ++stat_n_dom;
427 dominated = true;
428 break;
429 }
430 if (is_dominated<D, S>(cur, new_label)) { cur->set_dominated(true); }
431 } else if (cur_cost < cost_lo) {
432 if (dominates_resource_path<D, S>(new_label, cur)) {
433 ++stat_n_dom;
434 dominated = true;
435 break;
436 }
437 } else if (cur_cost > cost_hi) {
438 if (dominates_resource_path<D, S>(cur, new_label)) { cur->set_dominated(true); }
439 } else {
440 if (is_dominated<D, S>(new_label, cur)) {
441 ++stat_n_dom;
442 dominated = true;
443 break;
444 }
445 if (is_dominated<D, S>(cur, new_label)) { cur->set_dominated(true); }
446 }
447 }
448 }
449 if (profile_labeling) {
450 profile_record_dominance_check(D, S);
451 profile_record_inner_bin_scan(D, S, inner_scan_count);
452 if (D == Direction::Forward) {
453 dominance_checks_per_bucket[to_bucket] += static_cast<int>(inner_scan_count);
454 }
455 }
456 }
457
458 if (!dominated) {
459 if (profile_labeling) profile_record_non_dominated_label(D, S);
460 if constexpr (D == Direction::Forward) {
461 ++non_dominated_labels_per_bucket;
462 } else {
463 ++non_dominated_labels_per_bucket_bw;
464 }
465 ++n_labels;
466#ifdef SORTED_LABELS
467 mother_bucket.add_sorted_label(new_label);
468#elif defined(LIMITED_BUCKETS)
469 mother_bucket.sorted_label(new_label, BUCKET_CAPACITY);
470#else
471 mother_bucket.add_label(new_label);
472#endif
473 const int to_bucket_pos = bucket_pos_in_scc[to_bucket];
474 if (to_bucket_pos != -1) {
475 pending_labels_by_bucket[to_bucket_pos].push_back(new_label);
476 activate_bucket(to_bucket_pos);
477 }
478 }
479 }
480 }
481 label->set_extended(true);
482 }
483
484 bucket_is_active[bucket_idx] = 0;
485 }
486
487 for (const int bucket : scc_buckets) {
488 bucket_pos_in_scc[bucket] = -1;
489 buckets[bucket].compact_dominated_labels();
490 }
491
492 // Update c_bar values for all buckets in the current SCC. The topology
493 // builder already stores these buckets in direction-specific resource
494 // order, so avoid copying and sorting them on every labeling pass.
495 const auto &sorted_buckets = sorted_sccs[scc_index];
496
497 // Prefetch data for c_bar updates
498 if (!sorted_buckets.empty()) {
499 for (size_t i = 0; i < std::min(size_t(4), sorted_buckets.size()); ++i) {
500 __builtin_prefetch(&buckets[sorted_buckets[i]], 0, 3);
501 __builtin_prefetch(&Phi[sorted_buckets[i]], 0, 3);
502 }
503 }
504
505 // Batch processing of c_bar updates for improved cache locality.
506 for (size_t i = 0; i < sorted_buckets.size(); ++i) {
507 const int bucket = sorted_buckets[i];
508
509 // Prefetch data for next iterations
510 if (i + 4 < sorted_buckets.size()) {
511 __builtin_prefetch(&buckets[sorted_buckets[i + 4]], 0, 3);
512 __builtin_prefetch(&Phi[sorted_buckets[i + 4]], 0, 3);
513 }
514
515 double min_c_bar = buckets[bucket].get_cb();
516 const auto &phi_buckets = Phi[bucket];
517
518 // Prefetch first few phi buckets
519 if (!phi_buckets.empty()) {
520 for (size_t j = 0; j < std::min(size_t(4), phi_buckets.size()); ++j) {
521 __builtin_prefetch(&c_bar[phi_buckets[j]], 0, 3);
522 }
523 }
524
525 // Process phi buckets in blocks of 4 for better cache efficiency
526 size_t j = 0;
527 for (; j + 3 < phi_buckets.size(); j += 4) {
528 // Prefetch next block
529 if (j + 8 < phi_buckets.size()) {
530 __builtin_prefetch(&c_bar[phi_buckets[j + 4]], 0, 3);
531 __builtin_prefetch(&c_bar[phi_buckets[j + 5]], 0, 3);
532 __builtin_prefetch(&c_bar[phi_buckets[j + 6]], 0, 3);
533 __builtin_prefetch(&c_bar[phi_buckets[j + 7]], 0, 3);
534 }
535
536 // Compute min across 4 values at once
537 double val0 = c_bar[phi_buckets[j]];
538 double val1 = c_bar[phi_buckets[j + 1]];
539 double val2 = c_bar[phi_buckets[j + 2]];
540 double val3 = c_bar[phi_buckets[j + 3]];
541
542 min_c_bar = std::min(min_c_bar, std::min(std::min(val0, val1), std::min(val2, val3)));
543 }
544
545 // Process remaining phi buckets
546 for (; j < phi_buckets.size(); ++j) { min_c_bar = std::min(min_c_bar, c_bar[phi_buckets[j]]); }
547
548 c_bar[bucket] = min_c_bar;
549 }
550 }
551
552 // Store the best label from the labeling process.
553 if (Label *best_label_run = get_best_label<D>(topological_order, c_bar, sccs)) best_label = best_label_run;
554
555 return c_bar;
556}

◆ mono_initialization()

void BucketGraph::mono_initialization ( )

Initializes the BucketGraph for the mono-directional case.

584 {

590

593

596 bw_c_bar.resize(bw_buckets_size, std::numeric_limits<double>::infinity());

599 auto &num_bucket_index = assign_buckets<Direction::Forward>(num_buckets_index_fw, num_buckets_index_bw);

601 int num_intervals = options.main_resources.size();
602 std::vector<double> total_ranges(num_intervals);
603 std::vector<double> base_intervals(num_intervals);
604
605 auto &VRPNode = nodes[0]; // Example for the first node
606
607 // Calculate base intervals and total ranges for each resource dimension
608 for (int r = 0; r < intervals.size(); ++r) {
609 base_intervals[r] = (VRPNode.ub[r] - VRPNode.lb[r]) / intervals[r].interval;
610 }
611
612 // Clear forward and backward buckets
613 for (auto b = 0; b < fw_buckets.size(); b++) {
614 fw_buckets[b].clear();
615 bw_buckets[b].clear();
616 }
617
618 // Initialize forward buckets (generic for multiple dimensions)
619 std::vector<int> current_pos(num_intervals, 0);
620
621 std::vector<double> interval_starts(num_intervals);
622 std::vector<double> interval_ends(num_intervals);
623
624 // Initialize interval_starts to lower bounds and interval_ends to upper
625 // bounds
626 for (int r = 0; r < num_intervals; ++r) {
627 interval_starts[r] = VRPNode.lb[r];
628 interval_ends[r] = VRPNode.ub[r];
629 }
630 int offset_fw = 0;
631 int offset_bw = 0;
632
633 // Lambda to handle forward and backward direction initialization across
634 // combinations
635 auto initialize_intervals_combinations = [&](bool is_forward) {
636 int &offset = is_forward ? offset_fw : offset_bw;
637 auto &label_pool = is_forward ? label_pool_fw : label_pool_bw;
638 auto &buckets = is_forward ? fw_buckets : bw_buckets;
639 const auto &depot_id = is_forward ? options.depot : options.end_depot;
640 // print depot_id
641 int calculated_index_base =
642 is_forward ? num_buckets_index_fw[options.depot] : num_buckets_index_bw[options.end_depot];
643
644 std::vector<int> current_pos(num_intervals,
645 0); // Tracks current position in each interval dimension
646 std::vector<double> interval_bounds(num_intervals);
647
648 // Recursive lambda to generate all combinations of intervals
649 std::function<void(int)> generate_combinations = [&](int depth) {
650 if (depth == num_intervals) {
651 // All dimensions processed, now initialize for the current
652 // combination
653 auto depot = label_pool->acquire();
654 int calculated_index = calculated_index_base + offset;
655
656 // Set interval_bounds to current combination of interval
657 // starts or ends
658 for (int r = 0; r < num_intervals; ++r) {
659 interval_bounds[r] =
660 is_forward ? interval_starts[r] + current_pos[r] * roundToTwoDecimalPlaces(base_intervals[r])
661 : interval_ends[r] - current_pos[r] * roundToTwoDecimalPlaces(base_intervals[r]);
662 }
663
664 // print interval
665 // Initialize depot with the current interval boundaries
666 depot->initialize(calculated_index, 0.0, interval_bounds, depot_id);
667 depot->is_extended = false;
668 depot->cost += pstep_duals.getThreeTwoDualValue(depot_id);
669 depot->cost += pstep_duals.getThreeThreeDualValue(depot_id);
670 depot->addNode(depot_id);
671 set_node_visited(depot->visited_bitmap, depot_id);
672 SRC_MODE_BLOCK(depot->SRCmap.assign(cut_storage->activeSize(), 0);)
673 buckets[calculated_index].add_label(depot);
674 buckets[calculated_index].node_id = depot_id;
675
676 offset++; // Increment offset for each combination
677 return;
678 }
679
680 // Loop through each interval in the current dimension
681 for (int k = 0; k < intervals[depth].interval; ++k) {
682 current_pos[depth] = k;
683 generate_combinations(depth + 1);
684 }
685 };
686
687 // Start generating combinations from depth 0
688 generate_combinations(0);
689 };
690
691 // Call the lambda for both forward and backward directions, ensuring
692 // all combinations are processed
693 initialize_intervals_combinations(true); // Forward direction
694}
Here is the call graph for this function:

◆ ObtainJumpBucketArcs()

template<Direction D>
void BucketGraph::ObtainJumpBucketArcs ( )

Obtains jump bucket arcs for the BucketGraph.

This function iterates over each bucket in the BucketGraph and adds jump arcs to the set of buckets. It checks if each arc exists in the given Gamma vector for the current bucket. If an arc exists, it adds the corresponding bucket to the set of buckets to add jump arcs to. It then removes the non-component-wise minimal buckets from the set. Finally, it adds jump arcs from the current bucket to each bucket in the set.

Definition at line 355 of file BucketJump.h.

355 {
356 // Select direction-specific containers.
357 auto &buckets = assign_buckets<D>(fw_buckets, bw_buckets);
358 auto &num_buckets_idx = assign_buckets<D>(num_buckets_index_fw, num_buckets_index_bw);
359 auto &num_buckets = assign_buckets<D>(num_buckets_fw, num_buckets_bw);
360 auto &Phi = assign_buckets<D>(Phi_fw, Phi_bw);
361 auto &buckets_size = assign_buckets<D>(fw_buckets_size, bw_buckets_size);
362
363 int arc_counter = 0; // Count of jump arcs added
364 int missing_counter = 0; // Count of missing paths
365
366 // Clear generated jump arcs from the previous jump-arc build. Jump arcs
367 // are also mirrored as marked BucketArc entries for the labeler.
368 for (auto &node : nodes) { node.clear_jump_arcs<D>(); }
369 for (auto &bucket : buckets) { bucket.template clear_generated_jump_arcs<D>(); }
370
371 // Bucket ids encode kappa in the paper's order. Jump arcs satisfy
372 // b_base \prec b_jump in kappa order for both senses. For backward buckets
373 // this means jumping to a numerically larger kappa / lower physical upper
374 // bound, with Extend applying q <- min(q, u_jump).
375 const int loop_begin = 0;
376 const int loop_end = buckets_size;
377 const int loop_step = 1;
378 for (int b = loop_begin; b != loop_end; b += loop_step) {
379 std::vector<int> B_bar; // Temporary storage for valid adjacent
380 // bucket indices
381
382 const int current_node_id = buckets[b].node_id;
383 const auto &bucket_arcs = buckets[b].template get_bucket_arcs<D>();
384 const auto &orig_arcs = nodes[current_node_id].template get_arcs<D>();
385
386 // For each original arc from the current node...
387 for (const auto &orig_arc : orig_arcs) {
388 const int from_node = orig_arc.from;
389 const int to_node = orig_arc.to;
390 bool have_path = false;
391
392 // Check if the current bucket arcs contain the desired
393 // path.
394 for (const auto &gamma : bucket_arcs) {
395 if (is_bucket_fixed<D>(gamma.from_bucket, gamma.to_bucket)) { continue; }
396
397 const int from_node_b = buckets[gamma.from_bucket].node_id;
398 const int to_node_b = buckets[gamma.to_bucket].node_id;
399
400 if (from_node == from_node_b && to_node == to_node_b) {
401 have_path = true;
402 break; // Path found: no need to search further.
403 }
404 }
405
406 // If no valid path was found, try to obtain a jump arc.
407 if (!have_path) {
408 ++missing_counter;
409
410 // Determine bucket range for the current node.
411 const int start_bucket = num_buckets_idx[current_node_id];
412 const int node_buckets = num_buckets[current_node_id];
413 const auto &bucket_splits = assign_buckets<D>(fw_bucket_splits, bw_bucket_splits)[current_node_id];
414 const auto decode_pos = [&](int bucket_id) {
415 std::vector<int> pos(bucket_splits.size(), 0);
416 int local_bucket = bucket_id - start_bucket;
417 for (size_t r = 0; r < bucket_splits.size(); ++r) {
418 pos[r] = local_bucket % bucket_splits[r];
419 local_bucket /= bucket_splits[r];
420 }
421 return pos;
422 };
423 const auto current_pos = decode_pos(b);
424
425 auto is_jump_candidate = [&](const std::vector<int> &candidate_pos) {
426 bool strict = false;
427 for (size_t r = 0; r < candidate_pos.size(); ++r) {
428 if (candidate_pos[r] < current_pos[r]) return false;
429 if (candidate_pos[r] > current_pos[r]) strict = true;
430 }
431 return strict;
432 };
433
434 auto is_componentwise_closest = [&](const std::vector<int> &candidate_pos,
435 const std::vector<std::vector<int>> &all_positions) {
436 for (const auto &other_pos : all_positions) {
437 if (&other_pos == &candidate_pos) continue;
438 bool leq = true;
439 bool strict = false;
440 for (size_t r = 0; r < candidate_pos.size(); ++r) {
441 if (other_pos[r] > candidate_pos[r]) {
442 leq = false;
443 break;
444 }
445 if (other_pos[r] < candidate_pos[r]) strict = true;
446 }
447 if (leq && strict) return false;
448 }
449 return true;
450 };
451
452 std::vector<int> candidate_buckets;
453 std::vector<std::vector<int>> candidate_positions;
454 std::vector<std::array<double, R_SIZE>> candidate_res;
455 std::vector<double> candidate_costs;
456 candidate_buckets.reserve(node_buckets);
457
458 const int scan_begin = b + 1;
459 const int scan_end = start_bucket + node_buckets;
460 const int scan_step = 1;
461 for (int candidate_b = scan_begin; candidate_b != scan_end; candidate_b += scan_step) {
462 const auto candidate_pos = decode_pos(candidate_b);
463 if (!is_jump_candidate(candidate_pos)) continue;
464
465 const auto &candidate_arcs = buckets[candidate_b].template get_bucket_arcs<D>();
466 for (const auto &gamma_candidate : candidate_arcs) {
467 if (is_bucket_fixed<D>(gamma_candidate.from_bucket, gamma_candidate.to_bucket)) continue;
468
469 const int from_node_candidate = buckets[gamma_candidate.from_bucket].node_id;
470 const int to_node_candidate = buckets[gamma_candidate.to_bucket].node_id;
471 if (from_node == from_node_candidate && to_node == to_node_candidate) {
472 candidate_buckets.push_back(candidate_b);
473 candidate_positions.push_back(candidate_pos);
474 candidate_res.push_back(gamma_candidate.resource_increment);
475 candidate_costs.push_back(gamma_candidate.cost_increment);
476 break;
477 }
478 }
479 }
480
481 // Keep the closest component-wise candidates: minimal
482 // kappa-larger buckets. This is the paper's B-dot front; it is
483 // direction-independent because backward buckets were already
484 // built as u_v - kappa*d.
485 for (size_t i = 0; i < candidate_buckets.size(); ++i) {
486 if (!is_componentwise_closest(candidate_positions[i], candidate_positions)) continue;
487
488 const int candidate_b = candidate_buckets[i];
489 B_bar.push_back(candidate_b);
490 std::vector<double> res(candidate_res[i].begin(),
491 candidate_res[i].begin() +
492 static_cast<std::ptrdiff_t>(options.main_resources.size()));
493 const double cost = candidate_costs[i];
494
495 buckets[b].template add_jump_arc<D>(b, candidate_b, res, cost);
496 nodes[buckets[b].node_id].template add_jump_arc<D>(b, candidate_b, res, cost, orig_arc.to);
497 buckets[b].template add_bucket_arc<D>(b, candidate_b, res, cost, true, orig_arc.to);
498 ++arc_counter;
499 }
500 }
501 }
502 }
503
504 // Print summary of jump arcs added.
505 CONDITIONAL(D, print_info("[Fw] added {} jump arcs\n", arc_counter),
506 print_info("[Bw] added {} jump arcs\n", arc_counter));
507}
CONDITIONAL
#define CONDITIONAL(D, FW_ACTION, BW_ACTION)

◆ print_statistics()

void BucketGraph::print_statistics ( )

Prints the statistics of the bucket graph.

This function outputs a formatted table displaying various metrics related to the bucket graph. The table includes headers and values for forward and backward labels, as well as dominance checks. The output is color-coded for better readability:

  • Bold blue for metric names
  • Green for values (backward labels)
  • Reset color to default after each line

The table structure: +-------------------—+--------------—+--------------—+ | Metric | Forward | Backward | +-------------------—+--------------—+--------------—+ | Labels | <stat_n_labels_fw> | <stat_n_labels_bw> | | Dominance Check | <stat_n_dom_fw> | <stat_n_dom_bw> | +-------------------—+--------------—+--------------—+

Note: The actual values for the metrics (e.g., stat_n_labels_fw, stat_n_labels_bw, etc.) should be provided by the corresponding member variables or functions.

Definition at line 730 of file BucketGraph.cpp.

730 {
731 const char *blue_bold = "\033[1;34m"; // Bold blue for metrics
732 const char *green = "\033[32m"; // Green for values (backward labels)
733 const char *reset = "\033[0m"; // Reset color
734
735 // Print table header with horizontal line and separators
736 fmt::print("\n+--------------------------------------------------------+\n");
737 fmt::print("|{}{:<20}{}| {:<15} | {:<15} |\n", blue_bold, " Metric", reset, " Forward", " Backward");
738 fmt::print("+--------------------------------------------------------+\n");
739
740 // Print labels for forward and backward with bold blue metric
741 fmt::print("|{}{:<20}{}| {:<15} | {:<15} |\n", blue_bold, " Labels", reset, stat_n_labels_fw, stat_n_labels_bw);
742
743 // Print dominated forward and backward labels with bold blue metric
744 fmt::print("|{}{:<20}{}| {:<15} | {:<15} |\n", blue_bold, " Dominance Check", reset, stat_n_dom_fw, stat_n_dom_bw);
745
746 // Print the final horizontal line
747 fmt::print("+--------------------------------------------------------+\n");
748
749 if (options.profile_labeling) { print_labeling_profile(); }
750
751 fmt::print("\n");
752}

◆ process_all_resources()

template<Direction D, typename Gamma, typename VRPNode>
bool BucketGraph::process_all_resources ( std::vector< double > & new_resources,
const std::array< double, R_SIZE > & initial_resources,
const Gamma & gamma,
const VRPNode & theNode,
size_t N )

Processes all resources by iterating through them and applying constraints.

This function recursively processes each resource in the new_resources vector by calling process_resource for each index from I to N-1. If any resource processing fails (i.e., process_resource returns false), the function returns false immediately. If all resources are processed successfully, the function returns true.

Definition at line 112 of file BucketRes.h.

117 [[likely]] case ResourceType::Disposable:
118 if (!process_disposable_resource<D>(new_resources[I], initial_resources[I], gamma.resource_increment[I],
119 theNode.lb[I], theNode.ub[I])) {
120 return false;
121 }
122 break;
123
124 case ResourceType::NonDisposable:
125 if (!process_non_disposable_resource<D>(new_resources[I], initial_resources[I], gamma.resource_increment[I],
126 theNode.lb[I], theNode.ub[I])) {
127 return false;
128 }
129 break;
130
131 case ResourceType::Binary: new_resources[I] = process_binary_resource<D>(gamma.resource_increment[I]); break;
132
133 case ResourceType::Battery:
134 if (!process_battery_resource<D>(new_resources[I], initial_resources[I], theNode.is_station,
135 options.battery_capacity)) {
136 return false;
137 }
138 break;
139
140 case ResourceType::RechargeCount:
141 if (!process_recharge_count<D>(new_resources[I], initial_resources[I], theNode.is_station,
142 options.max_recharges)) {
143 return false;
144 }
145 break;
146
147 case ResourceType::RechargeTime:
148 if (!process_recharge_time<D>(new_resources[I], initial_resources[I], theNode.is_station,
149 options.max_recharge_time)) {
150 return false;
151 }
152 break;
153
154 case ResourceType::MTW:
155 if (!process_mtw_resource<D>(new_resources[I], initial_resources[I], gamma.resource_increment[I],
156 theNode)) {
157 return false;
158 }
159 break;
160 }
161 }
162 return true;
163}

◆ process_resource()

template<Direction D, typename Gamma, typename VRPNode>
bool BucketGraph::process_resource ( double & new_resource,
const std::array< double, R_SIZE > & initial_resources,
const Gamma & gamma,
const VRPNode & theNode,
size_t I )
constexpr

Processes a resource based on its disposability type and direction.

This function updates the new_resource value based on the initial resources, the increment provided by gamma, and the constraints defined by theNode. The behavior varies depending on the disposability type of the resource.

◆ relative_gap_to_incumbent()

double BucketGraph::relative_gap_to_incumbent ( double lower_bound) const

622 {
623 if (!std::isfinite(incumbent) || !std::isfinite(lower_bound)) return std::numeric_limits<double>::infinity();

625 return std::max(0.0, incumbent - lower_bound) / denom;

◆ reset_fixed()

void BucketGraph::reset_fixed ( )
inline

Resets all fixed arcs in the graph.

This function iterates over each row in the fixed_arcs matrix and sets all elements to 0. It effectively clears any fixed arc constraints that may have been previously set.

◆ reset_pool()

void BucketGraph::reset_pool ( )
inline

Resets the forward and backward label pools.

This function resets both the forward (label_pool_fw) and backward (label_pool_bw) label pools to their initial states. It is typically used to clear any existing labels and prepare the pools for reuse.

◆ run_labeling_algorithms()

template<Stage state, Full fullness>
void BucketGraph::run_labeling_algorithms ( std::vector< double > & forward_cbar,
std::vector< double > & backward_cbar )

Runs forward and backward labeling algorithms in parallel and synchronizes the results.

T#his function creates tasks for forward and backward labeling algorithms using the provided scheduling mechanism. The tasks are executed in parallel, and the results are synchronized and stored in the provided vectors.

Definition at line 1146 of file BucketSolve.h.

1146 {
1147 // Schedule the forward labeling algorithm task on the scheduler.
1148 auto forward_task = stdexec::schedule(bi_sched) |
1149 stdexec::then([&]() { return labeling_algorithm<Direction::Forward, state, fullness>(); });
1150
1151 // Schedule the backward labeling algorithm task on the scheduler.
1152 auto backward_task = stdexec::schedule(bi_sched) |
1153 stdexec::then([&]() { return labeling_algorithm<Direction::Backward, state, fullness>(); });
1154
1155 // Combine the forward and backward tasks.
1156 auto combined_work = stdexec::when_all(std::move(forward_task), std::move(backward_task)) |
1157 stdexec::then([&](auto forward_result, auto backward_result) {
1158 // Store the results into the provided vectors.
1159 forward_cbar = std::move(forward_result);
1160 backward_cbar = std::move(backward_result);
1161 });
1162
1163 // Wait for all tasks to complete.
1164 stdexec::sync_wait(std::move(combined_work));
1165}

169 std::vector<std::vector<int>> extended_bucket_graph = bucket_graph;

171 for (size_t i = 0; i < buckets.size(); ++i) {
172 const auto &phi_set = Phi[i];
173 if (phi_set.empty()) continue;
174 for (auto phi_bucket : phi_set) {
175 if (phi_bucket >= 0 && static_cast<size_t>(phi_bucket) < extended_bucket_graph.size()) {

181 SCC scc_finder;

185
186#ifdef VERBOSE

188 constexpr auto reset = "\033[0m";
189 fmt::print((D == Direction::Forward) ? "FW SCCs:\n" : "BW SCCs:\n");

196#endif
197
198 std::vector<std::vector<int>> ordered_sccs;
199 ordered_sccs.reserve(sccs.size());
200 for (int scc : topological_order) { ordered_sccs.push_back(sccs[scc]); }
201
202 auto sorted_sccs = sccs;
203 for (auto &scc : sorted_sccs) {
204 if constexpr (D == Direction::Forward) {
205 pdqsort(scc.begin(), scc.end(), [&buckets](int a, int b) { return buckets[a].lb < buckets[b].lb; });
206 } else {
207 pdqsort(scc.begin(), scc.end(), [&buckets](int a, int b) { return buckets[a].ub > buckets[b].ub; });
208 }
209 }
210
211 for (auto &node : nodes) {
212 if constexpr (D == Direction::Forward) {
213 node.fw_arcs_scc.resize(sccs.size());
214 } else {
215 node.bw_arcs_scc.resize(sccs.size());
216 }
217 }
218
219 auto process_bucket_arcs = [&](int bucket, int scc_index) {
220 int from_node_id = buckets[bucket].node_id;
221 VRPNode &node = nodes[from_node_id];
222 const auto &node_arcs = (D == Direction::Forward) ? node.fw_arcs : node.bw_arcs;
223 std::vector<Arc> &filtered_arcs =
224 (D == Direction::Forward) ? node.fw_arcs_scc[scc_index] : node.bw_arcs_scc[scc_index];
225
226 const auto &bucket_arcs = buckets[bucket].template get_bucket_arcs<D>();
227 for (const auto &arc : bucket_arcs) {
228 int to_node_id = buckets[arc.to_bucket].node_id;
229 auto it = std::find_if(node_arcs.begin(), node_arcs.end(),
230 [&to_node_id](const Arc &a) { return a.to == to_node_id; });
231 if (it != node_arcs.end()) { filtered_arcs.push_back(*it); }
232 }
233 };
234
235 int scc_ctr = 0;
236 for (const auto &scc : sccs) {
237 for (int bucket : scc) { process_bucket_arcs(bucket, scc_ctr); }
238 ++scc_ctr;
239 }
240
241 auto sortAndDedup = [](std::vector<Arc> &arcs) {
242 pdqsort(arcs.begin(), arcs.end(),
243 [](const Arc &a, const Arc &b) { return std::tie(a.from, a.to) < std::tie(b.from, b.to); });
244 auto last = std::unique(arcs.begin(), arcs.end(),
245 [](const Arc &a, const Arc &b) { return a.from == b.from && a.to == b.to; });
246 arcs.erase(last, arcs.end());
247 };
248
249 for (auto &node : nodes) {
250 if constexpr (D == Direction::Forward) {
251 for (auto &arcs : node.fw_arcs_scc) { sortAndDedup(arcs); }
252 } else {
253 for (auto &arcs : node.bw_arcs_scc) { sortAndDedup(arcs); }
254 }
255 }
256
257 std::vector<int> bucket_scc_rank(buckets.size(), -1);
258 for (size_t rank = 0; rank < ordered_sccs.size(); ++rank) {
259 for (int bucket : ordered_sccs[rank]) { bucket_scc_rank[bucket] = static_cast<int>(rank); }
260 }
261
262 UnionFind uf(ordered_sccs);
263 if constexpr (D == Direction::Forward) {
264 fw_ordered_sccs = ordered_sccs;
265 fw_topological_order = topological_order;
266 fw_sccs = sccs;
267 fw_sccs_sorted = sorted_sccs;
268 fw_bucket_scc_rank = std::move(bucket_scc_rank);
269 fw_union_find = uf;
270 } else {
271 bw_ordered_sccs = ordered_sccs;
272 bw_topological_order = topological_order;
273 bw_sccs = sccs;
274 bw_sccs_sorted = sorted_sccs;
275 bw_bucket_scc_rank = std::move(bucket_scc_rank);
276 bw_union_find = uf;
277 }

◆ set_adjacency_list()

template<Symmetry SYM>
void BucketGraph::set_adjacency_list ( )

Definition at line 687 of file BucketUtils.h.

687 {
688 // === Step 1: Clear Existing Arcs from Each Node ===
689 for (auto &node : nodes) { node.clear_arcs(); }
690
691 // === Step 2: MST-Based Clustering ===
692 MST mst_solver(nodes, [this](int from, int to) { return getcij(from, to); });
693 auto mst = mst_solver.compute_mst();
694
695 // Collect and sort MST edge weights.
696 std::vector<double> edge_weights;
697 edge_weights.reserve(mst.size());
698 for (const auto &[weight, from, to] : mst) { edge_weights.push_back(weight); }
699 pdqsort(edge_weights.begin(), edge_weights.end());
700
701 // Compute theta as the 90th percentile (scaled down by 100).
702 size_t p90_index = static_cast<size_t>(0.9 * edge_weights.size());
703 double theta = (edge_weights[p90_index]) / 100.0;
704 print_info("Computed theta: {}\n", theta);
705
706 // Cluster nodes and build mapping: job_id -> cluster_id.
707 auto clusters = mst_solver.cluster(theta);
708 print_info("Number of clusters: {}\n", clusters.size());
709 std::vector<int> job_to_cluster(nodes.size(), -1);
710 for (int cluster_id = 0; cluster_id < clusters.size(); ++cluster_id) {
711 for (int job : clusters[cluster_id]) { job_to_cluster[job] = cluster_id; }
712 }
713
714 // === Step 3: Precompute Resource Information ===
715 // Find the index for the "time" resource.
716 auto time_it = std::find(options.resources.begin(), options.resources.end(), "time");
717 const int time_resource_idx =
718 (time_it != options.resources.end()) ? std::distance(options.resources.begin(), time_it) : -1;
719
720 // === Step 4: Process Arcs for Each Node ===
721
722 // Helper lambda to compute resource increments.
723 // Depending on symmetry, consumption is either taken as-is or averaged.
724 auto compute_resource_increments = [&](const VRPNode &from_node, const VRPNode &to_node, double travel_cost,
725 std::vector<double> &inc) {
726 const int nres = options.resources.size();
727 if constexpr (SYM == Symmetry::Asymmetric) {
728 for (int r = 0; r < nres; ++r) {
729 inc[r] = (r == time_resource_idx) ? travel_cost + from_node.duration : from_node.consumption[r];
730 }
731 } else {
732 for (int r = 0; r < nres; ++r) {
733 inc[r] = (r == time_resource_idx) ? (from_node.duration / 2.0 + travel_cost + to_node.duration / 2.0)
734 : ((from_node.consumption[r] + to_node.consumption[r]) / 2.0);
735 }
736 }
737 };
738
739 // Lambda to add forward and reverse arcs from a given node.
740 auto add_arcs_for_node = [&](const VRPNode &curr_node) {
741 // Containers to accumulate arcs before insertion.
742 using ArcTuple = std::tuple<double, int, std::vector<double>, double>;
743 std::vector<ArcTuple> forward_arcs;
744 std::vector<ArcTuple> reverse_arcs;
745 forward_arcs.reserve(nodes.size());
746 reverse_arcs.reserve(nodes.size());
747
748 // Iterate over candidate next nodes.
749 for (const auto &next_node : nodes) {
750 // Skip if the candidate is a depot, the same node, or a pstep
751 // depot.
752 if (next_node.id == options.depot || curr_node.id == next_node.id ||
753 (options.pstep && (next_node.id == options.pstep_depot || next_node.id == options.pstep_end_depot))) {
754 continue;
755 }
756
757 double travel_cost = getcij(curr_node.id, next_node.id);
758 double cost_inc = travel_cost - next_node.cost;
759
760 // Skip self-loops (or if bucket indexes match; here using node
761 // id as bucket id).
762 if (curr_node.id == next_node.id) continue;
763
764 // Compute resource increments.
765 std::vector<double> res_inc(options.resources.size());
766 compute_resource_increments(curr_node, next_node, travel_cost, res_inc);
767
768 // Check feasibility: ensure resource consumption does not
769 // exceed next node's upper bound.
770 bool feasible = true;
771 for (int r = 0; r < options.resources.size(); ++r) {
772 if (numericutils::gt(curr_node.lb[r] + res_inc[r], next_node.ub[r])) {
773 feasible = false;
774 break;
775 }
776 }
777 if (!feasible) continue;
778
779 // Compute priorities based on cluster membership.
780 bool same_cluster = (job_to_cluster[curr_node.id] == job_to_cluster[next_node.id]);
781 double forward_priority = (same_cluster ? 5.0 : 1.0) + 1.E-5 * next_node.start_time;
782 double reverse_priority = (same_cluster ? 1.0 : 5.0) + 1.E-5 * curr_node.start_time;
783
784 // Collect arcs for forward and reverse directions.
785 forward_arcs.emplace_back(forward_priority, next_node.id, res_inc, cost_inc);
786 reverse_arcs.emplace_back(reverse_priority, next_node.id, res_inc, cost_inc);
787 }
788
789 // Insert the arcs into the corresponding nodes.
790 for (const auto &[priority, to_node, res_inc, cost_inc] : forward_arcs) {
791 nodes[curr_node.id].template add_arc<Direction::Forward>(curr_node.id, to_node, res_inc, cost_inc,
792 priority);
793 }
794 for (const auto &[priority, from_node, res_inc, cost_inc] : reverse_arcs) {
795 nodes[from_node].template add_arc<Direction::Backward>(from_node, curr_node.id, res_inc, cost_inc,
796 priority);
797 }
798 };
799
800 // Process each node, skipping the end depot.
801 for (const auto &node : nodes) {
802 if (node.id != options.end_depot) { add_arcs_for_node(node); }
803 }
804}

553 cost_inc); // Store the reverse arc
554 }
555
556 // Add forward arcs from the current node to its neighbors

574

578 }

1509 {
1510 int update_ctr = 0;
1511 ankerl::unordered_dense::set<int> updated_buckets;
1512
1513 // Iterate over all buckets and check if either forward or backward
1514 // bucket indicates a split is needed.
1515 for (size_t b = 0; b < fw_buckets.size(); ++b) {
1516 if (fw_buckets[b].shall_split || bw_buckets[b].shall_split) {
1517 const int inner_id = fw_buckets[b].node_id;
1518 // Skip depot buckets.
1519 if (inner_id == options.depot || inner_id == options.end_depot) { continue; }
1520 // If this node was already updated, skip it.
1521 if (updated_buckets.contains(inner_id)) { continue; }
1522 for (auto r = 0; r < options.resources.size(); ++r) {
1523 // If the bucket split value is already at maximum (100),
1524 // skip.(127)
1525 //
1526 if (fw_bucket_splits[inner_id][r] == 100) { continue; }
1527 // Update the bucket splits by doubling the current value,
1528 // capped at 100.
1529 const int update_target = std::min(fw_bucket_splits[inner_id][r] * 2, 100);
1530 fw_bucket_splits[inner_id][r] = update_target;
1531 bw_bucket_splits[inner_id][r] = update_target;
1532 updated_buckets.insert(inner_id);
1533 }
1534 update_ctr++;
1535 }
1536 }
1537
1538 // If we have reached a significant number of updates and we haven't
1539 // accumulated too many cuts, then update bucket splits.
1540 const int CHANGE_THRESHOLD = nodes.size() / 6;
1541 if (update_ctr >= CHANGE_THRESHOLD && cut_storage->size() < 40) {
1542 print_info("Updating bucket splits with {} changes\n", update_ctr);
1543
1544 // Redefine buckets in parallel for both forward and backward
1545 // directions.
1546 PARALLEL_SECTIONS(
1547 work, bi_sched,
1548 SECTION {
1549 // Forward bucket redefinition.
1550 define_buckets<Direction::Forward>();
1551 },
1552 SECTION {
1553 // Backward bucket redefinition.
1554 define_buckets<Direction::Backward>();
1555 });
1556
1557 // Regenerate arcs after updating bucket splits.
1558 generate_arcs();
1559
1560 // Process jump bucket arcs in parallel.
1561 PARALLEL_SECTIONS(
1562 workB, bi_sched, SECTION { ObtainJumpBucketArcs<Direction::Forward>(); },
1563 SECTION { ObtainJumpBucketArcs<Direction::Backward>(); });
1564
1565 return true;
1566 }
1567 return false;
1568 }

◆ solvePSTEP()

std::vector< Label * > BucketGraph::solvePSTEP ( PSTEPDuals & inner_pstep_duals)

Solves the PSTEP problem and returns a vector of labels representing paths.

This function performs the following steps:

  1. Resets the pool.
  2. Initializes the mono algorithm.
  3. Runs a labeling algorithm in the forward direction.
  4. Iterates through the forward buckets and computes new labels.
  5. Filters and collects labels that meet the criteria.
Returns
std::vector<Label*> A vector of pointers to Label objects representing the paths.

34 const int CHUNK_SIZE = 10;
35
36 std::vector<Label *> all_paths;
37 std::vector<double> all_costs;
38 std::vector<int> all_firsts, all_lasts;
39
40 // Mutex for shared resources
41 std::mutex cuts_mutex;
42
43 const int JOBS = nodes.size();
44 exec::static_thread_pool pool(std::thread::hardware_concurrency());
45 auto sched = pool.get_scheduler();
46
47 std::vector<std::pair<int, int>> tasks;
48 for (int i = 0; i < JOBS - 1; ++i) {
49 for (int j = 1; j < JOBS; ++j) {
50 if (i == j) continue;
51 tasks.emplace_back(i, j);
52 }
53 }
54 auto bulk_sender =
55 stdexec::bulk(stdexec::just(), (tasks.size() + CHUNK_SIZE - 1) / CHUNK_SIZE, // Calculate total chunks
56 [&](std::size_t chunk_idx) {
57 // Calculate indices i, j from chunk_idx
58 size_t start_idx = chunk_idx * CHUNK_SIZE;
59 size_t end_idx = std::min(start_idx + CHUNK_SIZE, tasks.size());
60 for (size_t task_idx = start_idx; task_idx < end_idx; ++task_idx) {
61 auto [i, j] = tasks[task_idx];
62 BucketOptions sub_options;
63 auto sub_bg = BucketGraph(nodes, R_max[0], 20);
64 sub_bg.set_distance_matrix(distance_matrix);
65 sub_options.depot = i;
66 sub_options.end_depot = j;
67 // print i and j
68 sub_options.pstep = true; // print depot and end_depot
69 sub_options.max_path_size = options.max_path_size;
70 sub_options.min_path_size = options.min_path_size;
71 sub_bg.setOptions(sub_options);
72 sub_bg.setPSTEPduals(inner_pstep_duals);
73 sub_bg.setup();
74 sub_bg.reset_pool();
75 sub_bg.mono_initialization();
76 // Solve and get the paths
77 std::vector<double> forward_cbar(sub_bg.fw_buckets.size());
78 forward_cbar = sub_bg.labeling_algorithm<Direction::Forward, Stage::Four, Full::PSTEP>();
79 std::vector<Label *> paths;
80 // print paths.size
81 for (auto bucket : std::ranges::iota_view(0, sub_bg.fw_buckets_size)) {
82 auto bucket_labels = sub_bg.fw_buckets[bucket].get_labels();
83 // print bucket_labels size
84 for (auto label : bucket_labels) {
85 if (!label) continue;
86 auto new_label = compute_mono_label(label);
87 if ((new_label->nodes_covered.size() > 1) &&
88 (new_label->nodes_covered.back() == sub_options.end_depot) &&
89 (new_label->nodes_covered.size() >= options.min_path_size &&
90 new_label->nodes_covered.size() <= options.max_path_size)) {
91 paths.push_back(new_label);
92 }
93 }
94 }
95
96 // Check if no paths are found
97 if (paths.empty()) return;
98
99 // Lock to protect shared resources
100 {
101 std::lock_guard<std::mutex> lock(cuts_mutex);
102 // Process the found paths
103 for (const auto &path : paths) { all_paths.push_back(path); }
104 }
105 // }
106 }
107 });
108
109 // Submit work to the thread pool and wait for completion
110 auto work = stdexec::starts_on(sched, bulk_sender);
111 stdexec::sync_wait(std::move(work));
112
113 return all_paths;
114}

376 fmt::print("Iteration {}\n", z);

382 // Convert three_two to vector of pairs
383 std::vector<std::pair<int, double>> three_two_tuples;
384 three_two_tuples.reserve(three_two_duals.size());
385 std::transform(three_two_duals.begin(), three_two_duals.end(), std::back_inserter(three_two_tuples),

388
389 // Convert three_three to vector of pairs
390 std::vector<std::pair<int, double>> three_three_tuples;
391 three_three_tuples.reserve(three_three_duals.size());
392 std::transform(three_three_duals.begin(), three_three_duals.end(), std::back_inserter(three_three_tuples),
393 [index = 0](double value) mutable { return std::make_pair(index++, value); });
394 inner_pstep_duals.setThreeThreeDualValues(std::move(three_three_tuples));
395
396 // Convert three_five to vector of pairs of pairs
397 std::vector<std::pair<std::pair<int, int>, double>> arc_duals_tuples;
398 size_t total_size = 0;
399 for (const auto &row : three_five_duals) total_size += row.size();
400 arc_duals_tuples.reserve(total_size);
401
402 for (size_t i = 0; i < three_five_duals.size(); ++i) {
403 for (size_t j = 0; j < three_five_duals[i].size(); ++j) {
404 if (i != j) { // Skip diagonal or add specific condition if needed
405 arc_duals_tuples.emplace_back(std::pair{i, j}, three_five_duals[i][j]);
406 }
407 }
408 }
409 inner_pstep_duals.setArcDualValues(std::move(arc_duals_tuples));
410
411 auto sub_paths = solvePSTEP(inner_pstep_duals);
412 // print sub_paths.size
413 auto paths_added = 0;
414 for (auto path : sub_paths) {
415 if (path->cost >= 0) { continue; }
416 paths_added++;
417 all_paths.push_back(path->getRoute());
418 all_costs.push_back(path->real_cost);
419 all_firsts.push_back(path->nodes_covered.front());
420 all_lasts.push_back(path->nodes_covered.back());
421 }
422 if (paths_added == 0) { break; }
423
424 duals = solveTSP(all_paths, all_costs, all_firsts, all_lasts, distance_matrix);
425 }
426 return {};
427}

auto BucketGraph::solveTSP ( std::vector< std::vector< uint16_t > > & paths,
std::vector< double > & path_costs,

Definition at line 236 of file BucketPSTEP.cpp.

238 {
239 auto n = cost_matrix.size();
240 MIPProblem model = MIPProblem("tsp", 0, 0);
241 if (first_time) { generateInitialPaths(n - 1, paths, path_costs, firsts, lasts, cost_matrix); }
242 // Define variables
243 int R = paths.size(); // Number of p-steps
244 std::vector<baldesVarPtr> x(R); // Binary variables for each p-step
245 std::vector<baldesVarPtr> u(n); // Continuous variables for each node
246
247 // Create binary variables x_r for each p-step r
248 for (int i = 0; i < R; ++i) {
249 // fmt::print("path_costs[{}] = {}\n", i, path_costs[i]);
250 std::string varname = "x_" + std::to_string(i);
251 double custo = path_costs[i];
252 x[i] = model.add_variable(varname, VarType::Continuous, 0.0, 1.0, custo);
253 }
254
255 // Create continuous variables u_i for each node i
256 for (int i = 0; i < n; ++i) {
257 auto name = "u_" + std::to_string(i);
258 u[i] = model.add_variable(name, VarType::Continuous, 0.0, n - 1, 0.0);
259 }
260 model.setObjectiveSense(ObjectiveType::Minimize);
261
262 // Constraints (3.3) and (3.4):
263 std::vector<baldesCtrPtr> three_two(n);
264 std::vector<baldesCtrPtr> three_three(n);
265 for (int i = 1; i < n - 1; ++i) {
266 LinearExpression in_constraint;
267 LinearExpression out_constraint;
268 for (int r = 0; r < R; ++r) {
269 if (firsts[r] == i) { in_constraint += x[r]; }
270 if (lasts[r] == i) { in_constraint -= x[r]; }
271 if (std::find(paths[r].begin(), paths[r].end(), i) != paths[r].end() && i != lasts[r]) {
272 out_constraint += x[r];
273 }
274 }
275 three_two[i] = model.add_constraint(in_constraint == 0, "33_" + std::to_string(i));
276 three_three[i] = model.add_constraint(out_constraint == 1, "34_" + std::to_string(i));
277 }
278
279 // Constraints (3.5): Avoid sub-tours using MTZ constraints
280 std::vector<baldesCtrPtr> three_five_constraints;
281 std::vector<std::vector<baldesCtrPtr>> three_five_constraints_matrix(n, std::vector<baldesCtrPtr>(n));
282 for (int i = 0; i < n - 1; ++i) {
283 for (int j = 1; j < n; ++j) {
284 if (i != j) {
285 LinearExpression r_sum;
286 for (int r = 0; r < R; ++r) {
287 for (int k = 0; k < paths[r].size() - 1; ++k) {
288 if (paths[r][k] == i && paths[r][k + 1] == j) { r_sum += x[r]; }
289 }
290 }
291 auto ctr = u[i] - u[j] + (n - 1) * r_sum <= (n - 2);
292 three_five_constraints_matrix[i][j] =
293 model.add_constraint(ctr, "mtz_" + std::to_string(i) + "_" + std::to_string(j));
294 }
295 }
296 }
297
298 for (int i = 1; i < n; i++) {
299 model.add_constraint(u[i] >= 0, "u_lb_" + std::to_string(i));
300 model.add_constraint(u[i] <= n - 1, "u_ub_" + std::to_string(i));
301 }
302
303#ifdef IPM
304 IPSolver *ipSolver = new IPSolver();
305 auto matrix = model.extractModelDataSparse();
306 ipSolver->run_optimization(matrix, 1e-2);
307
308 auto duals = ipSolver->getDuals();
309#else
310 auto duals = std::vector<double>(N_SIZE, 0.0);
311#endif
312 std::vector<double> three_two_duals(n, 0.0);
313 std::vector<double> three_three_duals(n, 0.0);
314 std::vector<std::vector<double>> three_five_duals(n, std::vector<double>(n + 1, 0.0));
315 for (int i = 1; i < n - 1; ++i) {
316 three_two_duals[i] = options.three_two_sign * duals[three_two[i]->index()];
317 three_three_duals[i] = options.three_three_sign * duals[three_three[i]->index()];
318 }
319
320 // Extract duals for three_five constraints
321 for (int i = 0; i < n - 1; ++i) {
322 for (int j = 1; j < n; ++j) {
323 if (i != j) {
324 three_five_duals[i][j] =
325 options.three_five_sign * (n - 1) * duals[three_five_constraints_matrix[i][j]->index()];
326 }
327 }
328 }
329#ifdef IPM
330 auto lp_obj = ipSolver->getObjective();
331#else
332 auto lp_obj = 0.0;
333#endif
334 fmt::print("LP Objective: {}\n", lp_obj);
335 GRBEnv &env = GurobiEnvSingleton::getInstance();
336 GRBModel *modelGRB = model.toGurobiModel(env).release(); // Release ownership from unique_ptr
337 // set model verbose
338 modelGRB->set(GRB_IntParam_OutputFlag, 0);
339 // set model type as 2
340 modelGRB->set(GRB_IntParam_Method, 2);
341
342 // convert x vars to int and solve again
343 for (int i = 0; i < R; ++i) {
344 modelGRB->getVarByName("x_" + std::to_string(i)).set(GRB_CharAttr_VType, GRB_BINARY);
345 }
346 modelGRB->update();
347 modelGRB->optimize();
348
349 if (modelGRB->get(GRB_IntAttr_Status) == GRB_OPTIMAL) {
350 fmt::print("Integer solution found with value: {}\n", modelGRB->get(GRB_DoubleAttr_ObjVal));
351 for (int i = 0; i < R; ++i) {
352 if (modelGRB->getVarByName("x_" + std::to_string(i)).get(GRB_DoubleAttr_X) > 0.5) {
353 fmt::print("x_{} = {}\n", i, modelGRB->getVarByName("x_" + std::to_string(i)).get(GRB_DoubleAttr_X));
354 // print path nodes
355 for (auto node : paths[i]) { fmt::print("{} ", node); }
356 fmt::print("\n");
357 }
358 }
359 }
360
361 return std::make_tuple(three_two_duals, three_three_duals, three_five_duals);
362}

◆ solveTSPTW()

auto BucketGraph::solveTSPTW ( std::vector< std::vector< uint16_t > > & paths,
std::vector< double > & path_costs,
std::vector< int > & firsts,
std::vector< int > & lasts,
std::vector< std::vector< double > > & cost_matrix,
std::vector< double > & service_times,
std::vector< double > & time_windows_start,
std::vector< double > & time_windows_end,
bool first_time = false )

Definition at line 429 of file BucketPSTEP.cpp.

433 {
434 auto n = cost_matrix.size();
435 MIPProblem model = MIPProblem("tsptw", 0, 0);
436
437 if (first_time) {
438 generateInitialPathsTSPTW(n - 1, paths, path_costs, firsts, lasts, cost_matrix, time_windows_start,
439 time_windows_end, service_times);
440 }
441
442 // Variables
443 int R = paths.size();
444 std::vector<baldesVarPtr> x(R); // Binary variables for p-steps
445 std::vector<baldesVarPtr> omega(n); // Continuous variables for accumulated time
446
447 // Create binary variables x_r for each p-step r
448 for (int i = 0; i < R; ++i) {
449 x[i] = model.add_variable("x_" + std::to_string(i), VarType::Binary, 0.0, 1.0, path_costs[i]);
450 }
451
452 // Create continuous variables ω_i for time accumulation
453 for (int i = 0; i < n; ++i) {
454 omega[i] = model.add_variable("omega_" + std::to_string(i), VarType::Continuous, 0.0, 1e8, 0.0);
455 }
456 model.setObjectiveSense(ObjectiveType::Minimize);
457
458 std::vector<baldesCtrPtr> three_two(n);
459 std::vector<baldesCtrPtr> three_three(n);
460 // Constraints (4.11): Flow balance for p-steps
461 for (int i = 1; i < n - 1; ++i) {
462 LinearExpression flow_balance;
463 for (int r = 0; r < R; ++r) {
464 if (firsts[r] == i) flow_balance += x[r];
465 if (lasts[r] == i) flow_balance -= x[r];
466 }
467 three_two[i] = model.add_constraint(flow_balance == 0, "flow_" + std::to_string(i));
468 }
469
470 // Constraints (4.12): Visit each node at most once
471 for (int i = 1; i < n - 1; ++i) {
472 LinearExpression visit_once;
473 for (int r = 0; r < R; ++r) {
474 if (std::find(paths[r].begin(), paths[r].end(), i) != paths[r].end()) { visit_once += x[r]; }
475 }
476 three_three[i] = model.add_constraint(visit_once <= 1, "visit_" + std::to_string(i));
477 }
478
479 // Constraint (4.13): Visit source exactly once
480 LinearExpression source_visit;
481 for (int r = 0; r < R; ++r) {
482 if (firsts[r] == 0) { source_visit += x[r]; }
483 }
484 model.add_constraint(source_visit == 1, "source");
485
486 // Constraints (4.14): Time accumulation with big-M
487 std::vector<baldesCtrPtr> three_five_constraints;
488 std::vector<std::vector<baldesCtrPtr>> three_five_constraints_matrix(n, std::vector<baldesCtrPtr>(n));
489 for (int i = 0; i < n - 1; ++i) {
490 for (int j = 1; j < n; ++j) {
491 if (i != j) {
492 double M_ij = 1e8;
493 LinearExpression time_acc;
494 for (int r = 0; r < R; ++r) {
495 for (size_t k = 0; k < paths[r].size() - 1; ++k) {
496 if (paths[r][k] == i && paths[r][k + 1] == j) { time_acc += x[r]; }
497 }
498 }
499 three_five_constraints_matrix[i][j] = model.add_constraint(
500 omega[j] - omega[i] - (cost_matrix[i][j] + service_times[i]) * time_acc - M_ij * time_acc >= -M_ij,
501 "time_" + std::to_string(i) + "_" + std::to_string(j));
502 }
503 }
504 }
505
506 // define omega[0] = 0
507
508 // Constraints (4.15): Time window constraints
509 for (int i = 1; i < n - 1; ++i) {
510 LinearExpression node_used;
511 for (int r = 0; r < R; ++r) {
512 if (std::find(paths[r].begin(), paths[r].end(), i) != paths[r].end()) { node_used += x[r]; }
513 }
514 model.add_constraint(omega[i] - time_windows_start[i] * node_used >= 0, "tw_lb_" + std::to_string(i));
515 model.add_constraint(omega[i] - time_windows_end[i] * node_used <= 0, "tw_ub_" + std::to_string(i));
516 }
517
518// Solve the model
519#ifdef IPM
520 IPSolver *ipSolver = new IPSolver();
521 auto matrix = model.extractModelDataSparse();
522 ipSolver->run_optimization(matrix, 1e-2);
523
524 // Extract solution and duals
525 auto duals = ipSolver->getDuals();
526#else
527 auto duals = std::vector<double>(N_SIZE, 0.0);
528#endif
529 std::vector<double> three_two_duals(n, 0.0);
530 std::vector<double> three_three_duals(n, 0.0);
531 std::vector<std::vector<double>> three_five_duals(n, std::vector<double>(n + 1, 0.0));
532 for (int i = 1; i < n - 1; ++i) {
533 three_two_duals[i] = options.three_two_sign * duals[three_two[i]->index()];
534 three_three_duals[i] = options.three_three_sign * duals[three_three[i]->index()];
535 fmt::print("Duals for node {}: 3.2 = {}, 3.3 = {}\n", i, three_two_duals[i], three_three_duals[i]);
536 }
537
538 // Extract duals for three_five constraints
539 for (int i = 0; i < n - 1; ++i) {
540 for (int j = 1; j < n; ++j) {
541 if (i != j) {
542 three_five_duals[i][j] =
543 options.three_five_sign * (n - 1) * duals[three_five_constraints_matrix[i][j]->index()];
544 }
545 }
546 }
547
548 // Solution output and return
549 // fmt::print("LP Objective: {}\n", ipSolver->getObjective());
550
551 GRBEnv &env = GurobiEnvSingleton::getInstance();
552
553 GRBModel *modelGRB = model.toGurobiModel(env).release();
554 // set model verbose
555 modelGRB->set(GRB_IntParam_OutputFlag, 1);
556 // set model type as 2
557 modelGRB->set(GRB_IntParam_Method, 0);
558
559 modelGRB->update();
560 modelGRB->optimize();
561
562 return std::make_tuple(three_two_duals, three_three_duals, three_five_duals);
563}

565 {
566 int JOBS = nodes.size();
567
568 // Initialize containers for storing paths, costs, firsts, and lasts
569 std::vector<std::vector<uint16_t>> all_paths;
570 std::vector<double> all_costs;
571 std::vector<int> all_firsts;
572 std::vector<int> all_lasts;
573
574 // extract service time from nodes
575 std::vector<double> service_times;
576 std::vector<double> time_windows_start(JOBS, 0.0);
577 std::vector<double> time_windows_end(JOBS, 10000.0);
578 for (int i = 0; i < JOBS; ++i) {
579 time_windows_start[i] = nodes[i].lb[0];
580 time_windows_end[i] = nodes[i].ub[0];
581 service_times.push_back(nodes[i].duration);
582 }
583 // Step 1: Solve the initial TSPTW using the previously defined method
584 auto duals = solveTSPTW(all_paths, all_costs, all_firsts, all_lasts, distance_matrix, service_times,
585 time_windows_start, time_windows_end, true);
586
587 // Step 2: Set up iteration for generating new paths using dual values
588 int max_pstep_iterations = 10;
589 for (int z = 0; z < max_pstep_iterations; ++z) {
590 fmt::print("--------------------------------------\n");
591 fmt::print("Iteration {}\n", z);
592
593 // Extract dual values for constraints
594 auto three_two_duals = std::get<0>(duals);
595 auto three_three_duals = std::get<1>(duals);
596 auto three_five_duals = std::get<2>(duals);
597 // auto arrival_time_duals = std::get<3>(duals);
598
599 PSTEPDuals inner_pstep_duals;
600
601 // Convert dual values for constraint (3.32)
602 std::vector<std::pair<int, double>> three_two_tuples;
603 three_two_tuples.reserve(three_two_duals.size());
604 std::transform(three_two_duals.begin(), three_two_duals.end(), std::back_inserter(three_two_tuples),
605 [index = 0](double value) mutable { return std::make_pair(index++, value); });
606 inner_pstep_duals.setThreeTwoDualValues(std::move(three_two_tuples));
607
608 // Convert dual values for constraint (3.33)
609 std::vector<std::pair<int, double>> three_three_tuples;
610 three_three_tuples.reserve(three_three_duals.size());
611 std::transform(three_three_duals.begin(), three_three_duals.end(), std::back_inserter(three_three_tuples),
612 [index = 0](double value) mutable { return std::make_pair(index++, value); });
613 inner_pstep_duals.setThreeThreeDualValues(std::move(three_three_tuples));
614
615 // Convert dual values for MTZ constraints (3.34) and time windows (3.35)
616 std::vector<std::pair<std::pair<int, int>, double>> arc_duals_tuples;
617 for (size_t i = 0; i < three_five_duals.size(); ++i) {
618 for (size_t j = 0; j < three_five_duals[i].size(); ++j) {
619 if (i != j) { arc_duals_tuples.emplace_back(std::pair{i, j}, three_five_duals[i][j]); }
620 }
621 }
622 inner_pstep_duals.setArcDualValues(std::move(arc_duals_tuples));
623
624 for (size_t i = 0; i < three_five_duals.size(); ++i) {
625 for (size_t j = 0; j < three_five_duals[i].size(); ++j) {
626 if (i != j) { // Skip diagonal or add specific condition if needed
627 arc_duals_tuples.emplace_back(std::pair{i, j}, three_five_duals[i][j]);
628 }
629 }
630 }
631 inner_pstep_duals.setArcDualValues(std::move(arc_duals_tuples));
632
633 // Step 3: Solve the subproblem to generate new p-steps
634 fmt::print("Solving PSTEP subproblem\n");
635 auto sub_paths = solvePSTEP(inner_pstep_duals);
636 fmt::print("Subproblem solved\n");
637
638 // Add the new paths to the existing list if valid
639 int paths_added = 0;
640 for (auto path : sub_paths) {
641 if (path->cost >= 0) { continue; }
642 paths_added++;
643 all_paths.push_back(path->getRoute());
644 all_costs.push_back(path->real_cost);
645 all_firsts.push_back(path->nodes_covered.front());
646 all_lasts.push_back(path->nodes_covered.back());
647 }
648
649 // If no new paths were added, stop the iteration
650 if (paths_added == 0) { break; }
651
652 // Step 4: Re-solve the TSPTW with the updated set of p-steps
653 duals = solveTSPTW(all_paths, all_costs, all_firsts, all_lasts, distance_matrix, service_times,
654 time_windows_start, time_windows_end);
655 }
656
657 return {};
658}

847 int num_intervals = options.main_resources.size();
848 std::vector<double> total_ranges(num_intervals);
849 std::vector<double> base_intervals(num_intervals);
850
851 // Determine the base interval and other relevant values for
852 // each resource
853 for (int r = 0; r < num_intervals; ++r) {
854 total_ranges[r] = R_max[r] - R_min[r];
855 base_intervals[r] = total_ranges[r] / intervals[r].interval;
856 }
857
858 int original_num_buckets = buckets_size;
859
860 // To track splits per node_id
861 ankerl::unordered_dense::map<int, int> splits_per_node;
862
863 // Loop until the second-to-last bucket to avoid
864 // out-of-bounds access
865 for (int i = 0; i < original_num_buckets - 1; ++i) {
866 const auto &bucket = buckets[i];
867
868 // Calculate mid-point for each dimension
869 std::vector<double> mid_point(bucket.lb.size());
870 if constexpr (D == Direction::Forward) {
871 for (int r = 0; r < bucket.lb.size(); ++r) { mid_point[r] = (bucket.lb[r] + base_intervals[r]) / 2.0; }
872 } else {
873 for (int r = 0; r < bucket.lb.size(); ++r) { mid_point[r] = (bucket.ub[r] - base_intervals[r]) / 2.0; }
874 }
875
876 // Create two new buckets by splitting at the mid-point
877 Bucket bucket1, bucket2;
878 if constexpr (D == Direction::Forward) {
879 bucket1 = Bucket(bucket.node_id, bucket.lb, bucket.ub);
880 bucket2 = Bucket(bucket.node_id, mid_point, bucket.ub);
881 } else {
882 bucket1 = Bucket(bucket.node_id, bucket.lb, bucket.ub);
883 bucket2 = Bucket(bucket.node_id, bucket.lb, mid_point);
884 }
885
886 // Manually shift elements to the right to make space
887 // for the new bucket
888 for (int j = original_num_buckets; j > i + 1; --j) { buckets[j] = buckets[j - 1]; }
889
890 // Insert the new bucket at position i+1
891 buckets[i + 1] = bucket2;
892
893 // Replace the current bucket with the first half
894 // (bucket1)
895 buckets[i] = bucket1;
896
897 // Track the number of splits for this node_id
898 splits_per_node[bucket.node_id]++;
899
900 // Since we added a new bucket at i+1, increment i to
901 // skip over the new bucket
902 ++i;
903 ++original_num_buckets; // Adjust the count to account
904 // for the new bucket
905 }
906
907 // Update the global bucket size
908 buckets_size = original_num_buckets;
909
910 // Now update the num_buckets_index for each node_id based
911 // on the final positions
912 int current_index = 0;
913 for (int i = 0; i < original_num_buckets; ++i) {
914 const auto &bucket = buckets[i];
915 if (num_buckets_index[bucket.node_id] == -1) { num_buckets_index[bucket.node_id] = current_index; }
916 current_index++;
917 }
918 }

95 {
96 if (result.skip_stage_transition) return;
97
98 switch (active_stage) {
99 case Stage::One:
100 if (result.best_reduced_cost >= -1 || iter >= 10) { activate_bucket_pricing_stage(Stage::Two); }
101 break;
102 case Stage::Two:
103 if (result.best_reduced_cost >= -10 || iter > 500) { activate_bucket_pricing_stage(Stage::Three); }
104 break;
105 case Stage::Three:
106 if (result.best_reduced_cost >= -0.5) {
107 activate_bucket_pricing_stage(Stage::Four);
108 transition = true;
109 }
110 break;
111 case Stage::Four: {
112#ifdef FIX_BUCKETS
113 if (transition) { break; }
114#endif
115 if (status != Status::Rollback && considerRegenerate()) {
116 status = Status::Rollback;
117 break;
118 }
119 threshold = stats.computeThreshold(iter, result.best_reduced_cost);
120 constexpr double kExactPricingTol = 1e-6;
121 if (result.best_reduced_cost >= -kExactPricingTol) {
122 ss = true;
123#if !defined(SRC) && !defined(SRC3)
124 status = Status::Optimal;
125#else
126 status = Status::Separation;
127#endif
128 } else {
129 ss = false;
130 status = Status::NotOptimal;
131 }
132 break;
133 }
134 case Stage::Enumerate:
135 if (result.enumeration_failed) {
136 activate_bucket_pricing_stage(Stage::Four);
137 status = Status::NotOptimal;
138 } else {
139 status = Status::Optimal;
140 }
141 break;
142 default:
143 break;
144 }
145}

◆ UpdateBucketsSet()

template<Direction D>
void BucketGraph::UpdateBucketsSet ( double theta,
const Label * label,
ankerl::unordered_dense::set< int > & Bbidi,
int & current_bucket,
std::vector< uint64_t > & Bvisited )

Updates the set of buckets based on the given parameters.

This function inserts the current bucket into the visited set and then performs various checks and updates based on the provided theta, label, Bbidi, current_bucket, and Bvisited parameters.

Definition at line 24 of file BucketJump.h.

25 {
26 // Select direction-specific data.
27 auto &Phi_opposite = assign_buckets<D>(Phi_bw, Phi_fw);
28 auto &buckets_opposite = assign_buckets<D>(bw_buckets, fw_buckets);
29 auto &c_bar_opposite = assign_buckets<D>(bw_c_bar, fw_c_bar);
30
31 // Cache label values to avoid repeated member lookups.
32 const int bucketLnode = label->node_id;
33 const double label_cost = label->cost;
