36
37 // Precompute a flag for direction (avoid repeated ternary operators).
38 constexpr bool forward = (D == Direction::Forward);
39
40 // Lambda to check if the visited bitmaps of L and L_bw overlap.
41 auto overlapsVisited = [n_bitmap, this](const Label *L, const Label *L_bw) -> bool {
42 // Make a local copy of the visited bitmap.
43 auto visited_bitmap = L->visited_bitmap;
44
45 // check if visited_bitmap overlaps at all
46 for (size_t i = 0; i < n_bitmap; ++i) {
47 if ((visited_bitmap[i] & L_bw->visited_bitmap[i]) != 0) { return true; }
48 }

67
68 // Use a local DFS stack to traverse buckets.
69 static thread_local std::vector<int> bucket_stack;
70 bucket_stack.clear();
71 bucket_stack.reserve(32);
72 bucket_stack.push_back(current_bucket);
73
74 // Process buckets until the stack is empty.
75 while (!bucket_stack.empty()) {
76 const int curr_bucket = bucket_stack.back();
77 bucket_stack.pop_back();
78
79 // Skip if already visited.
80 const size_t segment = curr_bucket >> 6;
81 const size_t bit_pos = curr_bucket & 63;
82 if ((Bvisited[segment] & (1ULL << bit_pos)) != 0) { continue; }
83
84 // Mark the current bucket as visited.
85 Bvisited[segment] |= (1ULL << bit_pos);
86
87 // Get the opposite bucket’s label and node id.
88 const int bucketLprimenode = buckets_opposite[curr_bucket].node_id;
89 double cost = getcij(bucketLnode, bucketLprimenode);
90
91 // Optionally adjust cost with arc duals if RCC mode is enabled.
92 RCC_MODE_BLOCK(auto arc_dual = arc_duals.getDual(bucketLnode, bucketLprimenode); cost -= arc_dual;)
93
94 // Skip if the accumulated cost plus lower bound exceeds theta.
95 if (numericutils::gt(label_cost + cost + c_bar_opposite[curr_bucket], theta)) continue;
96
97 const auto &opposite_labels = buckets_opposite[curr_bucket].get_labels();
98 for (const auto &L_opposite : opposite_labels) {
99 if (L_opposite == nullptr || L_opposite->is_dominated) continue;
100
101 // Skip labels from the same node.
102 if (bucketLnode == L_opposite->node_id) continue;
103
104 if constexpr (D == Direction::Forward) {
105 if (overlapsVisited(label, L_opposite)) continue;
106 } else {
107 if (overlapsVisited(L_opposite, label)) continue;
108 }
109
110 // Determine the reference node for resource checks.
111 const VRPNode &ref_node = forward ? nodes[bucketLnode] : nodes[L_opposite->node_id];
112
113 bool violated = false;
114 const size_t num_resources = options.resources.size();
115 for (size_t r = 0; r < num_resources; ++r) {
116 if (options.resources[r] == "time") {
117 const double time_constraint = forward
118 ? label->resources[TIME_INDEX] + cost + ref_node.duration
119 : L_opposite->resources[TIME_INDEX] + cost + ref_node.duration;
120 if (numericutils::gt(time_constraint,
121 forward ? L_opposite->resources[TIME_INDEX] : label->resources[TIME_INDEX])) {
122 violated = true;
123 break;
124 }
125 } else {
126 const double resource_constraint = forward ? label->resources[r] + ref_node.consumption[r]
127 : L_opposite->resources[r] + ref_node.consumption[r];
128 if (numericutils::gt(resource_constraint,
129 forward ? L_opposite->resources[r] : label->resources[r])) {
130 violated = true;
131 break;
132 }
133 }
134 }
135
136 if (violated) continue;
137
138 // Record only buckets that have at least one theta-compatible
139 // label pair.
140 if (numericutils::lt(label_cost + cost + L_opposite->cost, theta)) {
141 Bbidi.emplace(curr_bucket);
142 break;
143 }
144 }
145
146 // Add unvisited neighbor buckets.
147 for (int b_prime : Phi_opposite[curr_bucket]) {
148 const size_t seg_prime = b_prime >> 6;
149 const size_t bit_prime = b_prime & 63;
150 if ((Bvisited[seg_prime] & (1ULL << bit_prime)) == 0) bucket_stack.push_back(b_prime);
151 }
152 }
153}

◆ updateSplit()

void BucketGraph::updateSplit ( )
inline

Definition at line 1033 of file BucketGraph.h.

1033 {
1034 // Base thresholds and parameters for adjustments.
1035 constexpr double BASE_IMBALANCE_THRESHOLD = 0.10; // Lower threshold —
1036 // work-weighted signal
1037 // is more sensitive
1038 // than raw counts.
1039 constexpr double MIN_ADJUSTMENT_FACTOR = 0.002;
1040 constexpr double MAX_ADJUSTMENT_FACTOR = 0.015;
1041 constexpr double EMA_ALPHA = 0.1; // Smoothing factor for EMA.
1042 constexpr int SPLIT_UPDATE_COOLDOWN = 5;
1043
1044 // Prefer a work-weighted signal: total labels scanned during dominance
1045 // walks captures both check count and per-bin depth, which is what the
1046 // labeler actually pays for. Fall back to label counts when the
1047 // profiler hasn't recorded data yet (first iteration / profiling off).
1048 // Sum across stages because Stage 1-3 may be active early and Stage 4
1049 // dominates later iterations.
1050 uint64_t fw_work = 0, bw_work = 0;
1051 for (size_t i = 0; i < PROFILE_STAGE_COUNT; ++i) {
1052 fw_work += profile_inner_bin_scan_labels_fw[i];
1053 bw_work += profile_inner_bin_scan_labels_bw[i];
1054 }
1055 const bool have_work_signal = (fw_work + bw_work) > 0;
1056 const double fw_labels = have_work_signal ? static_cast<double>(fw_work) : static_cast<double>(n_fw_labels);
1057 const double bw_labels = have_work_signal ? static_cast<double>(bw_work) : static_cast<double>(n_bw_labels);
1058 const double total_labels = fw_labels + bw_labels;
1059
1060 // Skip adjustment if too few labels exist.
1061 if (total_labels < 10) return;
1062
1063 // Compute imbalance ratios for forward and backward labels.
1064 const double fw_ratio = fw_labels / total_labels;
1065 const double bw_ratio = bw_labels / total_labels;
1066 const double imbalance_ratio = std::abs(fw_ratio - bw_ratio);
1067
1068 // Update exponential moving average (EMA) of the imbalance ratio.
1069 split_imbalance_ema = EMA_ALPHA * imbalance_ratio + (1.0 - EMA_ALPHA) * split_imbalance_ema;
1070
1071 // Keep a real dead-band even on large passes. Letting the threshold
1072 // decay to zero makes q_star chase noise and creates concat churn.
1073 const double dynamic_threshold =
1074 std::max(0.03, BASE_IMBALANCE_THRESHOLD * std::exp(-total_labels / 50000.0));
1075 if (iter - last_split_update_iter < SPLIT_UPDATE_COOLDOWN) return;
1076
1077 // Only adjust split if the imbalance exceeds the dynamic threshold.
1078 if (split_imbalance_ema > dynamic_threshold) {
1079 // Severity scales between 0 and 1.
1080 const double severity = (split_imbalance_ema - dynamic_threshold) / (1.0 - dynamic_threshold);
1081
1082 // Adaptive adjustment factor increases with severity.
1083 const double adjustment_factor =
1084 MIN_ADJUSTMENT_FACTOR + (MAX_ADJUSTMENT_FACTOR - MIN_ADJUSTMENT_FACTOR) * severity;
1085
1086 // Compute resource range based on the main resource.
1087 const double resource_range = R_max[options.main_resources[0]] - R_min[options.main_resources[0]];
1088
1089 // Base adjustment scales with the resource range; no decay so
1090 // adjustments stay aggressive when imbalance persists late.
1091 const double base_adjustment = adjustment_factor * resource_range;
1092 last_split_update_iter = iter;
1093
1094 // Adjust each split position in q_star.
1095 for (size_t i = 0; i < q_star.size(); ++i) {
1096 // Compute relative position (0: min, 1: max) within the
1097 // resource range.
1098 const double relative_pos = (q_star[i] - R_min[options.main_resources[0]]) / resource_range;
1099
1100 // Position factor gives smaller adjustments near boundaries.
1101 const double position_factor = 4.0 * relative_pos * (1.0 - relative_pos);
1102
1103 // Determine direction: shift toward balancing label counts.
1104 const double direction = (bw_labels > fw_labels) ? 1.0 : -1.0;
1105
1106 // Final adjustment for this split.
1107 const double final_adjustment = base_adjustment * position_factor * direction;
1108
1109 // Apply the adjustment with boundary protection.
1110 q_star[i] = std::clamp(q_star[i] + final_adjustment,
1111 R_min[options.main_resources[0]] + resource_range * 0.1, // Lower bound.
1112 R_max[options.main_resources[0]] - resource_range * 0.1 // Upper bound.
1113 );
1114 }
1115
1116 // Log adjustments if the base adjustment is significant.
1117 if (std::abs(base_adjustment) > resource_range * 0.01) {
1118 print_info("Split adjustment: imbalance={:.3f}, adjustment={:.3f}\n", split_imbalance_ema,
1119 base_adjustment);
1120 }
1121 }
1122 }
